lakes.name: Abernathy, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5-1/2E, Sec 2 2006:  Hike-in lake, 1 mile in Willamette National Forest. From Eugene, take Hwy 58 east to Odell Lake. From West Bay on the lake, take Forest Rd. 5899 which parallels the railroad to the old station of Abernethy. Take Deer Creek Trail 3670 south. (Oregon Fish Finder) 2011:  From Abernathy SnoPark, follow dirt road and turn right on Spur Rd 501.  Keep right at split.  Travel 3.0 miles to end of road at Deer Ck.  Trail head is across Deer Ck near railroad.  Old trail head appeared to have crossed no-longer-existing bridge across Deer Ck upstream of end of road.   A mile-long developed Forest Service trail climbs uphill to Lower and Upper Abernathy Lakes from the end of the road.

lakes.name: Aerial
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 27 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Highway (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake.  The Island Meadows Trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of Elk Lake. From Island Meadows Trailhead, continue west to the Pacific Crest Trail. Turn south on the Pacific Crest Trail and continue to junction of USFS Trail 3517.  Continue northwest on USFS Trail 3517 to McBee Trail (USFS Trail 3523); continue northeast (right turn) on USFS Trail 3523 to Herb Lake (located on the right side of the trail).  Aerial Lake is approximately 200 yards southeast of Herb Lake.  Suggest compass and good map.

lakes.name: Alameda
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5E, Sec 25 1989:  Two miles west of Summit Lake immediately north of Emigrant Butte.  No trail to lake or identifying sign.  Hike 300 yards up a dry (or nearly dry) small creek channel to the lake from Forest Service Road 391 at a point 0.3 mile south of its junction with Road 380.  (Don't confuse with a larger creek just 0.1 mile from this junction.)  An alternate route of about the same hiking distance can be taken downhill from Forest Service Road 393 south of the lake.

lakes.name: Alta Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Alton Baker Canal
lakes.description: Alton Baker Pond and Canoe Canal are located within Alton Baker Park, one of the most popular and multi-functional parks in Eugene. The park is located on the north bank of the Willamette River just east of the Ferry Street Bridge and Coburg Road. It is accessible by foot or bike from either side of the river via the River Bank Trails. By car you can enter via the Club Road underpass off Coburg Road or from Autzen Stadium off MLK/Centennial. Located with in the park are two ponds and a canal, which can be accessed by rented canoes during the summer months.

lakes.name: Amstutz
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R8E, Sec 28 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Eugene, take Highway 126 east; turn right on to Foley Ridge Road (USFS Road 2643) and follow USFS Road 2643 to the end.  Take USFS Trail 3511 for approximately 8 miles, then head north on USFS Trail 3547 for 1 mile to reach Eileen Lake.  Amstutz Lake is located about ½ mile northwest of Eileen Lake at the top of the steep, rocky slope overlooking Eileen.

lakes.name: Ana Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 60-acrereservoir located about 23 miles northeast of Paisley, Oregon. This reservoirmaintains a temperature of about 58 degrees and is open year around. It isstocked with hybrid bass and rainbow trout. Boats can be launched from animproved ramp and an accessible dock is available. Many anglers target hybridbass January to March using crankbaits (e.g. Rapala’s) from the shore, butthese fish can be caught anytime during the year. Anglers using boats can trollcrankbaits or jig for hybrid bass. Check the regulations for special conditionsthat exist for harvesting hybrid bass. Trout fishing can be good anytime of theyear because water temperatures keep them active. Bait, lures, flies, ortrolling will be productive for trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Ana River
lakes.description: This is a spring-fed river that begins at the Ana Reservoir dam and continues through Summer Lake Wildlife Area (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/summer_lake_wildlife_area.asp). Sampling has identified substantially higher numbers of trout in the first 5.5 miles or the river. The water temperature remains about 58 degrees and is optimal for rainbow trout. Bait can be used on this river. Bait, lures, and flies are all effective methods for catching rainbow trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Andrea
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S R04E  Sec 35 Forest Service Road #23 to 2308, Spur 429 (Groundhog Creek/Hills Creek).

lakes.name: Andrews
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R4E, Sec 3 Andrews Lake is a small lake in Section 03 which is shown at the head of Cedar Creek on the Willamette National Forest maps.  However, it is not located on the creek, just in its headwaters, and its winter overflow, if any, probably drains eventually into Cedar Creek.

lakes.name: Antelope Flat Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 170-acre reservoir located in the Maury Mountain area of the Ochoco National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gjAwhwtDDw9_AI8zPyhQoY6BdkOyoCAGixyPg!/?ss=110601&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&cid=fsbdev3_036040&navid=091000000000000&pnavid=null&position=Welcome.Html&ttype=detail&pname=Deschutes%20&%20Ochoco%20National%20Forests%20&%20Crooked%20River%20National%20Grassland-%20Home), 30 miles southeast of Prineville. Antelope Flat Reservoir Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recarea/?recid=38784) and boat ramp are located at the west end of the reservoir. This a very productive reservoir with excellent opportunities for rainbow trout 14-18 inches. Flies, lures and bait are all effective. Bank angling opportunities are present, however, anglers in boats or float tubes tend to be more successful. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Anvil
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Anvil Lake Trail #724

lakes.name: Applegate Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 988-acre reservoir in southwestern Oregon just north of the California border. The lake was created in 1980 by damming the mainstream of the Applegate River 47 miles upstream from its confluence with the Rogue. It is named after Lindsay Applegate, a prominent early Oregon pioneer. This lake is located in the Skskiyou Mountains within the Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/rogue-siskiyou/). This is a flood control reservoir and depth varies dramatically between spring and fall. The lake is popular for cycling, boating, canoeing, fishing and hiking the various trails near the lake. The elevation of the lake is 1,987 feet. Camping: Camping is available at Hart-Tish (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea?recid=70232&actid=29),and Watkins (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea?recid=70404&actid=29) campgrounds. Dispersed hike-in camping is also available around the reservoir at French Gulch, Latgawa Cove, Streamtown, Harr Point and Tipsu Tyee campgrounds. Seattle Bar is a day-use only area at the south end of the reservoir. Jackson Picnic Park and Flumet Flat campgrounds are located about 5.6 miles downstream from the reservoir. Fees at some locations. Private concessionaire available seasonally. Boaters are subject to a 10 m.p.h. speed limit. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Arizona Pond
lakes.description: This is a one-acre pond located at Arizona Beach State Park Recreation Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_258.php) approximately 15 miles south of Port Orford (http://www.portorford.org/). It is stocked with catchable and trophy trout in the spring and early summer. Fishing can be good all summer, depending on water conditions and stocking level. The pond has good bank access and a great place for kids to fish. The pond is managed for youths 17 and under. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Arrowhead Lake
lakes.description: This small, two acre lake is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Averill
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Red Lake Trail #719

lakes.name: Badger Lake
lakes.description: This is a small lake located on the southeast slope of Mt. Hood, within the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood). Access to this lake is via a steep, primitive forest road that is usually closed during the winter months. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Baker Pond
lakes.description: This is a 2-acre pond located in the White River Wildlife Area (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/white_river_wildlife_area.asp) in Wasco County about 10 miles south of Dufur, Oregon. This spring-fed lake is stocked in the spring and generally open from April through October. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Beal Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Bell Lake
lakes.description: Bell Lake is  small, 2 acre lake with a maximum depth of 34 feet. Bell Lake is stocked with rainbow and brook trout

lakes.name: Ben Irving Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 250-acre lake in Douglas County, Oregon, created by the construction of Berry Creek Dam, which impounds Berry Creek. The reservoir is named for a longtime Oregon outdoorsman and is located in a narrow canyon about 10 miles southwest of Winston, Oregon. Boats are allowed on the reservoir, which is popular for water skiing, butthere is an electric motor only section for angling. Amenities: boat ramp, picnic tables, barbecue, restroom Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Benson
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36. 2012:  A 20-acre lake located in the Mtn. Washington Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene take Highway 126, east (past the community of McKenzie Bridge) to McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242). Turn northeast on Hwy 242; continue on to USFS Road 260 to Scott Lake; from Scott Lake Campground take USFS Trail 3502 (for approximately 1.5 miles) to Benson Lake.

lakes.name: Benson Lake
lakes.description: This is a 40-acre lake located in Benson State Park in the Columbia River Gorge. From Portland, head east on I-84, park is located on the south side of the freeway approx. 1/2 mile west of Multnomah Falls. Benson State Recreation Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_147.php)

lakes.name: Bethany Pond
lakes.description: This is a 10-acre pond located at Bethany west of Portland. The pond is maintained by Tualatin Hills Park and Rec. (http://www.thprd.org/parks/parkdetail.cfm?id=23) Amenities include picnic tables, restrooms, and a paved, ADA accessible trail.

lakes.name: Betty
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec. 8 2012:  A 40-acre lake located in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 to Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897).  Betty Lake Trailhead is on the west side of Waldo Lake Road (USFS Rd 5897) approximately 6 miles from Hwy 58. The Betty Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3664) extends about ½ mile to Betty Lake and continues onto Waldo Lake.

lakes.name: Beulah Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 2,060-acre irrigation reservoir located on the North Fork Malheur River 19 miles north of Juntura, Oregon. It is open year round to trout fishing. The reservoir supports populations of bull trout, redband trout, and whitefish. The resevoir is stocked in the spring when enough water is present to support trout into the fall. Camping sites exist at the boat ramp and along the eastern shore. Best fishing occurs in the spring from ice out until June. Fishing improves again in the fall after water temperatures cool Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Big Creek Reservoir #1
lakes.description: This is a 20-acre reservoir on Big Creek Road northeast of Newport, offering year-round fishing.

lakes.name: Big Creek Reservoir #2
lakes.description: The larger of the two reservoirs on Big Creek Road northeast of Newport, offering year-round fishing.

lakes.name: Big Cultus Lake
lakes.description: This is a 785-acre lake located about 50 miles south of Bend, Oregon in the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon). This lake is deep and holds lake trout in addition to stocked rainbows. There is a resort (http://www.cultuslakeresort.com/) on the southeast shore of the lake, with restaurant, boat and motor rentals, cabins, supplies and gas. Cultus Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38836&actid=29) is a large campground with sites for RVs up to 30 feet. For campers who want more privacy West Cultus Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39028&actid=29) offers boat-in only campsites. In addition to fishing, activities at Big Cultus Lake include swimming, water skiing and hiking. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Big Lava Lake
lakes.description: This is a 160-acre lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) approximately 38 miles south of Bend, Oregon. This beautiful spring-fed lake reaches a maximum depth of about 30 feet and supports populations of brook trout and rainbow trout. Camping and a boat ramp are available at Lava Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38894&actid=29) on the south shore. There is also a private resort with RV hook-ups, boat rentals and supplies. Boats are subject to a 10 m.p.h. speed limit. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Big Rock Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 5-acre reservoir located 15 north of Plush, Oregon within the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php). This reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually and has the capacity to grow fish up to 16”. The reservoir can summer kill (algae bloom) occasionally as happened in 2011. The reservoir was re-stocked in 2012. This is a great little reservoir to float tube and the fish are very willing to bite because they don’t get much fishing pressure. The reservoir does not show up on current BLM maps, but is located in Mary Ann Draw. Use BLM Road #6105 to access Mary Ann Draw. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Big Slide
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Bull of the Woods Wilderness. Big Slide Lake Trail #553A

lakes.name: Big Slide, Upper
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Bull of the Woods Wilderness. Big Slide Lake Trail #553A

lakes.name: Bikini Pond
lakes.description: This is a 4-acre pond next to the Columbia River in Mayer State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_161.php) between Hood River and The Dalles, Oregon. There is a picnic area, accessible restrooms, and paved parking area. Fishing is from the bank, although the park also provides access to the Columbia River. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Birthday
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 23 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to USFS Road 5883; continue on USFS Road 5883 to road’s end and trailhead.  This is the trailhead for Fuji Mountain and Island Lakes.  Take USFS Trail 3674 to Lower Island Lake.  Birthday Lake is less than ½ mile south on the same trail.

lakes.name: Blair
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5E, Sec 17 1988:  Forest Service gravel roads join and extend to the lake from both the Salmon Creek and North Fork Willamette River road.  (Refer to Forest Service maps which show roads and road  numbers).  Small boats can be carried about 100 feet to the lake from the parking area at the road deadending at the lake.  Motors on boats are allowed but seldom used.  Drinking water (pump), picnic tables and several low-development camp sites are at the lake.

lakes.name: Blow Lake
lakes.description: Forty five acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Blue
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5E, Sec 34 2006:  Hike-in lake, half a mile into Diamond Peak Wilderness Area in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, take Hwy 58 southeast to Hills Creek Reservoir turn-off. Follow Hwy 21 around the reservoir and up the upper Middle Fork Willamette, about 26 miles, to Forest Rd. 2145. After about 5 miles, turn east onto Forest Rd. 2149. Blue Lake Trailhead is 4 miles up this road. Hike is a gradual uphill of about one mile. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Blue Canyon Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Blue Lake
lakes.description: This is a 64-acre lake located in Blue Lake Park 3 miles west of Troutdale. This family-friendly park as picnic areas, restrooms, walking trail, and ramp for small boats. Park is maintained by Multnomah County. Blue Lake Regional Park (http://www.oregonmetro.gov/index.cfm/go/by.web/id=149)

lakes.name: Blue Lake
lakes.description: This is a 20-acre lake located in the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) in the Gearheart Mountain Wilderness (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=60171&actid=34). The lake is accessed via a 3-mile trail off of NF-3372. The trailhead is about equal distances from Bly, Paisley, or Lakeview. This lake makes a great day trip or overnight camping excursion. Fingerling rainbow trout are stocked annually with mules and help from the High Desert Trail Riders. Bait, flies, and lures all work well for trout from 6”-16”. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Bluebill Lake
lakes.description: This lake is located within the Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72DTgBAjAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAObEnGA!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!?navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&recid=42465&actid=&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&ss=110612&position=&ttype=recarea&pname=Siuslaw%2520National%2520Forest%2520-%2520Oregon%2520Dunes%2520National%2520Recreation%2520Area) about 3 miles north of North Bend, Ore. Nearby Bluebill Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42647&actid=31&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Bluebill+Campground) has camping sites for tents, cars, RVs and trailers. A one-mile hiking trail circles the lake. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Boat
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 8 1988:  In Three Sisters Wilderness near Beaver Marsh.  Where Trail 3523 crosses the inlet (not outlet) stream entering Beaver Marsh from the northwest, walk up the creek 300 yards to Boat Lake; the lake can't be seen from this or other trails and has no identifying sign or trail access.

lakes.name: Bobby Lake
lakes.description: Eighty five acre lake with max depth of 85 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Bolan Lake
lakes.description: Description: 13 acres surface area on USFS property Amenities: primitive boat ramp. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Bongo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 2 2006:  Hike-in lake, half a mile west of the southern tip of Waldo Lake in Waldo Lake Wilderness of Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 about 23 miles southeast. Follow Forest Rd 5897 to the northeast, then northwest on 5896 to Shadow Bay Campground. Take Trail 3590 south to Trail 3572 which leads to Bingo Lake. Bushwhack less than a mile northwest of Bingo to Bongo. A map and compass/GPS are advised.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Bonnie Lake
lakes.description: Bonnie is a six acre lake stocked with brook trout. Maximum depth of 24 feet.

lakes.name: Boo Boo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 27 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 to USFS Road 5894 (one mile east of Salt Creek Falls).  From USFS Road 5894 proceed northeast to trailhead; hike approximately 1½ miles up steep South Waldo Trail (USFS Trail 3586) to Boo Boo Lake.

lakes.name: Boot
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 15 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.   From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Highway (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake; continue approximately 1 mile south of Elk Lake to the Doris Lake Trailhead.  Continue on Doris Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3526) to Pacific Crest Trail.  Continue south on the Pacific Crest Trail. When you reach Cliff Lake, go to the west shore and forge path through brush for ½ mile due east to Boot Lake.

lakes.name: Booth Lake
lakes.description: Booth Lake spans eight acres with a maximum depth of 34 feet. It is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Bradley Lake
lakes.description: This is a 30-acre lake about three miles north of Bandon, Ore. ODFW owns a boat ramp. Bank fishing is limited because most of the lakeshore is privately owned. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Brahma Lake
lakes.description: Ten acre lake stocked with cutthroat trout.

lakes.name: Breitenbush River - Breitenbush Campground
lakes.description: Primitive U.S. Forest Service campground located next to the Breitenbush River, located about 10 miles northeast of Detroit. Amenities: picnic tables, toilets, drinking water fire grills, garbage service, 30 sites, 5 accessible sites. Breitenbush Campground Map (http://www.fs.usda.gov/detailfull/willamette/recreation/camping-cabins/?cid=stelprdb5136312&width=full)

lakes.name: Breitenbush River - Bridge at USFS Rd 4685
Breitenbush River - Cleator Bend Campground
lakes.description: This small group campground is located on a bend in the Breitenbush River. From Detroit, go 9 miles east on Breitenbush Road #46; campground is in a wooded area and has nine campsites. Open May-September, maximum stay is 14 days. Fee. Amenities include restrooms,picnic tables, drinking water and fire grills. The Breitenbush River runs through a beautiful, narrow canyon with crystal clear water.

lakes.name: Breitenbush River Bridge
lakes.description: A tributary to Detroit Reservoir. This site is at a bridge over the Breitenbush River. Fish are released directly from the bridge. There is a small amount of gravel parking next to the bridge but it is very limited.

lakes.name: Brittany
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 5 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west.  After 1 mile, turn north to reach the Rigdon Lakes.  Forge a path through brush due east from the northeast corner of Lower Rigdon Lake to reach Brittany Lake.

lakes.name: Brown, North
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Brown, South
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Buck
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Buck Lake Trail #728

lakes.name: Buck Lake
lakes.description: A 5-acre lake about 4.5 miles north of Florence, Ore.. A trail on the west side of Hwy. 101 leads a few hundred feet to the lake.

lakes.name: Buckeye Lake
lakes.description: This is a 10-acre hike-in lake located within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua/). There are improved campgrounds nearby at Buckeye and Cliff Lake. The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Bug
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5E, Sec 16 1988:  Located in Middle Fork Willamette River drainage about 200 yards uphill from Forest Service Road 2144 about 1.3 mile northeast of Spur Road 327.  No access trail or identifying sign.  Can't be seen from Road 2144, but is visible downhill from the North Umpqua Divide Road 0.6 mile to the south.  A Forest Service map showing locations of the lake and nearby clearcuts is helpful.

lakes.name: Bull Pup Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This lake is located in the North Umpqua watershed within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) east of Roseburg, Ore. The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Bully Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 1,001-acre irrigation reservoir located about 9.5 miles west of Vale Oregon via Grand Blvd and Bully Creek Road. This reservoir is managed primarily for warmwater fish, but on dry years rainbow trout have been stocked into the reservoir when most other water bodies are dry. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Bump
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Burma Pond (Secesh Reservoir)
lakes.description: This is a 4.5-acre pond located approximately 8 miles from the community of Wolf Creek in Josephine County, Oregon. There is easy access along the bank side of this pond. Only non-motorized boats are allowed. Due to the steepness of BLM Road #33-5-15.2, the last half mile to the site, four-wheel-drive is recommended. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Burns Pond
lakes.description: This is a small pond located on the south side of Hwy. 78 approximately two miles east of Burns, Ore. The pond has good access and is sometimes used for family fishing events. Facilities include picnic tables and an accessible fishing dock. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Burnt
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood Wilderness. Burnt Lake Trail #772

lakes.name: Burnt Top
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 2 2012:  A 20-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Highway (Hwy 46); about 5 miles past Bachelor Butte and just past Devil's Lake Campground, go west to Sister's Mirror Lake on USFS Trail 3527.  Preferred access is using a compass and a good map to find where to forge a path through the brush to the south a quarter mile from the trail to the Burnt Top Lake.

lakes.name: Butterfield Lake
lakes.description: This 50-acre lake is located about six miles north of Coos Bay, and just west of Highway 101 in the Riley Ranch Campground (http://www.co.coos.or.us/Departments/CoosCountyParks/RileyRanch.aspx) operated by Coos County Parks (http://www.co.coos.or.us/Departments/CoosCountyParks.aspx). An access road from the campground to the lake allows trout stocking trucks to reach the lake, and provides access for anglers to launch small “cartop” boats and canoes. Fishing docks also provide angler access. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Cabot Lake
lakes.description: A small, six acre lake stocked with rainbow trout. Natural reproduction of brook trout.

lakes.name: Calamut Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is an 18-acre hike-in Lake located within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua/). There are a few improved campsites at the lake. The lake is stocked annually with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Campbell Lake
lakes.description: Thisis a 21-acre lake located in a high mountain setting on the Fremont-WinemaNational Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) approximately 30 miles southeast of Paisley, Oregon.Located at an elevation of 7,195 ft., this lake is not accessible until late-Junebecause of snow. The lake is stocked annually with legal- and trophy-sizedrainbow trout. Small boats can be launched from a boat ramp at Campbell Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59755&actid=29) but are restricted toelectric motors and a 5 m.p.h. speed limit. Camping is also available nearby atDead Horse Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59761&actid=29). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Canby Pond
lakes.description: This 1-acre pond is located on the south end of Canby, in Canby City Park. Canby Pond is open only to youth 17 years old and under and persons who possess ODFW's Disabled Hunting and Fishing Permits. Amenities: picnic area, restrooms.

lakes.name: Cape Meares Lake
lakes.description: A 65-acre lake located 7 miles west and north of Tillamook, Ore., along Bay Ocean Rd.

lakes.name: Cardiac
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 26 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24), continue on USFS Road 24 to junction; continue to the left onto Black Creek Road (USFS Road 2421).  Travel ½ mile, turn left onto USFS Road 2422 and follow road to end. Trailhead begins at the end of USFS Road 2422. At the trailhead, the easiest access is by forging a path through brush a mile south and east, using a compass and good map.

lakes.name: Carey Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Carl Lake
lakes.description: Located in the Mt. Jefferson Wilderness, Carl Lake is small, six acre lake with a maximum depth of 51 feet.  The lake has naturally reproducing brook trout and is stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Carter Lake
lakes.description: A long, narrow 28-acre lake that lies parallel to and west of Hwy.101. Good bank access, protection from the wind, campground with easy access to dune.

lakes.name: Cast
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood Wilderness. Cast Lake Trail #796

lakes.name: Charlton Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with rainbow trout. Short hike to lake.

lakes.name: Chetlo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 12 2012:  A 20-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west, and then USFS Trail 3583 south. Chetlo Lake is easily visible from the trail.

lakes.name: Chewaucan River
lakes.description: The river has mixed private-U.S. Forest Service ownership but has many public access points on the tributaries and mainstem river. The river is managed for native redband trout and has multiple campgrounds to stage from. Special harvest and gear regulations are in effect on the river. Native redband trout range from 10-20+ inches. The lower river (below Hwy 31) has largemouth bass near Rivers End Reservoir, a private reservoir. Most bass are 6”-10” long, and brown bullhead catfish are also present, but small. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Chickahominy Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 530-acre reservoir in Oregon's high desert, located approximately 32 miles west of Burns. It is heavily stocked with fingerling trout in the spring and is known for producing some large rainbows. There is a boat ramp, and many anglers troll for trout while others fish from the shore. There is a boat ramp on the southeast end of the reservoir, accessed by a gravel road. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Clare
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7.5E, Sec 16 2012:  A 14-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east to McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242). Turn east on Hwy 242 and continue past Scott Lake Campground for approximately 1 mile to the Hand Lake Trailhead (USFS Trail 3513); take the Hand Lake Trail to Hand Lake.  Easiest route is to hike north 1 ½ miles from Hand Lake on the Hand Lake Trail, then strike out northeast to the lava flow and follow it 1 to 2 miles to Clare Lake.

lakes.name: Clear
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Clear Lake
lakes.description: This is a beautiful lake located within the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood) on the east side of Mt. Hood, about 12 miles from Government Camp, Oregon. A boat ramp, picnic area and campsites are located at Clear Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/wateractivities/recarea/?recid=53058&actid=78) on the east side of the lake. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Clearwater Number Two Forebay - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is one of two forebays in the area and is the only one that is part of ODFW's trout stocking program. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Cleawox Lake
lakes.description: Cleawox lake is 87 acres in size and is one of the most popular lakes on the Oregon Coast. Its defining features are the large sand dunes that form its southern and western shores. From these dunes one can gain access to the Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area. Most of the shoreline is in public ownership with easy bank access. The elevation is near sea level. Honeyman State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_134.php) More information (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/fish/easy_angling/northwest/cleawox.asp)

lakes.name: Cliff
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 16 2012:  A 17-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Highway (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake;  from  Hwy 46, continue approximately 1 mile south of Elk Lake to the Doris Lake Trailhead; travel west on Doris Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3526) to Pacific Crest Trail; continue southwest on the Pacific Crest Trail. Cliff Lake is located on the east side of the Pacific Crest Trail near Mink Lake.

lakes.name: Cliff Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a 7-acre hike-in lake located within the Umpqua National Forest in the Rogue Umpqua Divide Wilderness. (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua). Skimmerhorn Trail 1578 offers the most direct way in. Campsites are nearby at Cliff and Buckeye. The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWhich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Cliff Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Coffenbury Lake
lakes.description: A 50-acre lake located in Fort Stevens State Park 2 miles west of Warrenton. Improved boat ramp, docks and good bank access. More information (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/fish/easy_angling/northwest/coffenbury.asp) Fort Stevens State Park (http://oregonstateparks.org/park_179.php)

lakes.name: Commonwealth Lake
lakes.description: This is a three-acre stocked lake within the Commonwealth Lake Park (http://www.thprd.org/parks/parkdetail.cfm?id=53) in Beaverton. Commonwealth Park is maintained by Tualatin Hills Park and Rec. Amenities (http://www.thprd.org/parks/parkdetail.cfm?id=53) include ADA accessible trail, picnic tables, playground, restrooms.

lakes.name: Connie Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a small lake in the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) that is accessible by 4-wheel-drive.  The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWhich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Cooper Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 160-acre reservoir located about three miles east of Sutherlin, Oregon in the Umpqua River Basin. Boating is allowed and the reservoir is popular for waterskiing but there are still several fingers good for fishing as well as a speed limit zone near the boat ramp. Amenities: two boat ramps, pavilion, picnic area, restroom, barbecue Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Copepod
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 9 2012:  A 7-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Highway (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake; continue approximately 1 mile south of Elk Lake to the Doris Lake Trailhead (USFS Trail 3526); continue west on USFS Trail 3526.  USFS Trail 3526 will “zigzag” across the Pacific Crest Trail and continue on to Vera Lake (south side of trail); continue approximately 200 feet farther to Questionmark Lake (north side of trail), and then on to Copepod Lake.

lakes.name: Corner
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 8 1988:  In Three Sisters Wilderness about 1-1/2 mile northeast of Mink Lake.  Popular Forest Service trails passes within 250 feet of the west and south shores; the lake is easily visible from these trails.  Probably no identifying sign.

lakes.name: Corrigan
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5E, Sec 3 2012:  A 6-acre lake located in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Kitson Springs Road; continue on Kitson Springs Road for 0.5 miles to USFS Road 21; Continue on USFS Road 21 for approximately 30 miles to junction with USFS Road 2149.  Take a left onto USFS Road 2149 and continue on to the Corrigan Lake Trailhead (USFS Trail 3654).  Hike uphill approximately 1-½ miles on USFS Trail 3654 to Corrigan Lake.

lakes.name: Cottage Grove Ponds
lakes.description: Row River Nature Park Ponds, also known as Cottage Grove Ponds, is a 15-acre site with six ponds. Stocking schedule -- https://myodfw.com/fishing/species/trout/stocking-schedule?field_zone_value=6&field_waterbody_name_value_1=ROW+R+NATURE+PK-FMR+COT.GRV+PD&field_planned_stocking_date_value%5Bdate%5D=2019-01-01&field_planned_stocking_date_end_value%5Bdate%5D=2019-12-31

lakes.name: Cottage Grove Reservoir
lakes.description: A 1.150-acre reservoir located six miles south of Cottage Grove, Oregon. This reservoir has four day-use only parks, three on the lakeshore and one on the river immediately below the dam. All are open mid-May to mid-September. Lakeside Park is located on London Road just past the dam. The park has paved roads and parking lots, flush toilets, picnic tables, fire rings and a year-around boat ramp. Wilson Creek Park is on Reservoir Road at the southwest end of the lake, and has paved roads and parking lots, flush toilets, picnic tables, fire rings, a children's play area, unsupervised swim area and courtesy boat dock. Riverside Park is on the east bank of the river, downstream from the dam. This primitive park has a gravel road and parking area, two picnic tables and a vault toilet. A paved path with pull-outs suitable for wheelchairs is close enough to the river for fishing.

lakes.name: Cottonwood Meadows
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cottonwood Meads Trail #705

lakes.name: Cottonwood Meadows Lake
lakes.description: Thisis a 42-acre lake located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) about 22 miles northwest ofLakeview, Oregon. It is located at an elevation of 6,130 feet and subject to heavy snowfall. The lake is open year-round but access may at times be limited to snowmobile, snow shoes or dog sleds. This lake is stockedannually with rainbow and brook trout persist through natural production. Thereare two campgrounds on the lake, and two cement boat ramps, although motors arerestricted to electric only. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Craig
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7.5E, Sec 24 2006:  Hike-in lake, 3 miles into the southern Mt. Jefferson Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Santiam Pass on Hwy 20 take the Pacific Crest Trail to Trail 3491 and follow it north  about a mile. Look for a fair trail up the northeast side of Craig Butte - about half a mile to the lake on top.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Crane Prairie Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 5-square-mile reservoir located in the heart of one of Oregon's richest wildlife areas. The reservoir ranges from 10-20 feet deep and is a premier fishery for large rainbow trout and brook trout. Largemouth bass and kokanee are also available. Hatchery released rainbow trout are all marked with clipped adipose fins. Harvest is limited to five trout per day with only one fish over 20 inches, wild rainbow trout must be released. Large numbers of brook trout are available, with the best fishing early and late in the season. Fish for brook trout at dawn and dusk with flies, lures and bait. Anglers after bigger brook trout should cast their fly, lure or bait close to cover such as submerged logs or undercut banks. Crane Prairie offers fair to good largemouth bass fishing opportunities, which improve as water temperature increase. These fish typically range from 2-7 pounds. There are good boat ramps at Rock Creek, Quinn River Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!?ss=110601&ttype=recarea&recid=38958&actid=29&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Central+Oregon+-+Quinn+River+Campground) and Crane Prairie Resort (http://www.crane-prairie-resort-guides.com/). The resort offers lodging, meals and supplies. Motorized boats are allowed but are restricted to 10 m.p.h. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Crawfish
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R1E, Sec 28 1988:  In Brice Creek drainage southeast of Cottage Grove on Umpqua National Forest land.  Located downhill from (to north of) Forest Service Road 2234.  A developed trail extends from this road about 1/4 mile to the lake; a trailhead probably exists on the road.  The lake can be seen from the road but may not be easily visible in the future when vegetation grows higher in the clearcut between the road and lake.

lakes.name: Crescent Lake
lakes.description: This is a 6-square-mile lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) about 75 miles south of Eugene, Oregon. Open year-around, this large, deep lake produces lake trout ranging from 5 to 20 pounds.Crescent Lake also has a good kokanee fishery, producing fish in the 12-13" range. The lake is regularly stocked with brown and rainbow trout. Camping is available aty Crescent Lake (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39060&actid=29), Spring (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39102&actid=29) and Contorta Point (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39052&actid=29) campgrounds. Picnic facilities are available at Simax Beach, Tandy Bay and Tranquil Cove. Cabins are available for rent at Crescent Lake Resort (http://crescentlakeresort.com/), which also has a restarant, store, and boat rentals. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Cub
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. A bushwacking adventure.

lakes.name: Cullaby Lake
lakes.description: A 200-acre lake located appr. 4 miles north of Gearhart, Ore., and east of Hwy 101. Cullaby Lake County Park  has 165 acres, offering public access to Cullaby Lake boat launch and docks, four-stall flush restrooms, picnic shelters, barbecue pits, and horseshoe pits. Activities include fishing, swimming, wildlife viewing of beavers, bald eagles, deer, and waterfowl.

lakes.name: Dan
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 5 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel the east side of Waldo Lake;  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west; after approximately one mile cut north on USFS Trail 3555 to Lower Rigdon Lake.  Easiest access is to walk northeast from northeast corner of Lower Rigdon Lake (from a point where the shoreline trail leaves the lake shore and heads toward Kiwa Lake) up a steep hill 300 yards to Dan Lake.  A compass and good map are recommended.

lakes.name: Darlene Lake
lakes.description: Eleven acre lake, max depth 48 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: David
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719.

lakes.name: Deadhorse Lake
lakes.description: This is a 31-acrelake located in a high mountain setting on the Fremont-WinemaNational Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) approximately 30 miles southeast of Paisley, Oregon.Located at an elevation of 7,372 ft., this lake is not accessible until late-Junebecause of snow. The lake is stocked annually with legal- and trophy-sizedrainbow trout throughout the summer. Small boats can be launched but arerestricted to electric motors and a 5 m.p.h. speed limit. Camping is availablenearby at Deadhorse Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/wateractivities/recarea/?recid=59761&actid=78). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Dee
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5.5E, Sec 16 1989:  Located 3/4 mile west of Lower Timpanogas Lake.  No trail or identifying sign.  From the northeast corner of a large clearcut at the end of Spur Road 241, hike east (uphill) about 200 yards to the lake.  This spur road is extremely rough and ends in the center of the clearcut.

lakes.name: Deer Camp
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 15 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Eagle Creek Road (USFS Road 5883); follow USFS Road 5883 until you reach USFS Spur Road 381; continue to end of USFS Spur Road 381; from the end of USFS Spur Road 381 follow trail to Divide Trail (USFS Trail 3572).  Deer Camp Lake is 100 yards downhill from USFS Trail 3572.  Deer Camp Lake can be seen through trees from Divide Trail.

lakes.name: Delintment Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 50-acre reservoir located in the Ochoco National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) approximately 47 miles northwest of Burns, Oregon. This lake is situated within a ponderosa pine forest and offers good bank access. Boats are allowed on the reservoir but are restricted to a 5 m.p.h. speed limit. Fishing is best in the spring and early summer, as the reservoir can become choked with aquatic vegetation in the summer. A large campground with overnight camping, day-use area, boat lauch and accessible boat dock is located on-site. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Dennis Lake
lakes.description: Eleven acre lake with max depth of 42 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Detroit Lake - Hoover Campground
lakes.description: Hoover Campground rests at an elevation of 1,600 feet on the shores of Detroit Lake. Camp sites are surrounded by towering trees, making for secluded camping spots. There are several hiking trails in the area, including Stahlman Point Trail, which offers beautiful vistas of the lake and surrounding mountain area. There is a boat ramp adjacent to the campground . Additional amenities include: ADA fishing platform, camp sites, vault toilet, grills, paved parking lot. Reservations are available  May through September.

lakes.name: Detroit Lake - Kane's Marina
lakes.description: Kane’s Marina is located on Detroit Lake 50 miles east of Salem. Detroit Lake is a prime recreation area easily accessible from both eastern Oregon and the Willamette Valley. At 1,600 feet above sea level, Detroit Lake is fed by clear mountain water that warms to perfect temperature for swimming, skiing, wakeboarding, tubing and other in-the-water activities during the summer months. Kane’s Marina (http://www.kanesmarina.com/) is a private facility that offers 400 slips for seasonal and overnight moorage, 200 ft. of guest tie-up, seasonal and overnight RV spaces, boat rentals, fuel, and convenience store where fishing licenses and tackle are sold.

lakes.name: Detroit Lake - Mongold Day Use Area
lakes.description: Mongold Day Use Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_91.php) is 1.5 miles west of Detroit Lake State Park. It's the only public boat launch facility on the lake featuring a swimming area, grass beach, picnic facilities and restrooms. Mongold is a year-round fee area. Mongold has 58 single car parking spaces and three fully-accessible parking spaces near the swimming area, and another 25 single car parking spaces at the top of the parking lot. There are 120 vehicle/trailer parking spaces, and three that are accessible to people with disabilities.

lakes.name: Detroit Lake State Park
lakes.description: Located in the Cascade Mountains, Detroit Lake State Park is the gateway to beautiful Detroit Lake. The 400-foot-deep lake was created in 1953 when the U.S. Army Corp of Engineers completed the Detroit Dam project. The lake is over nine miles long with more than 32 miles of shore line. It is popular for fishing, boating, swimming, water skiing, and personal watercraft. The park has a large campground bordering the lake. Along with two swimming areas, there are two playgrounds, a wildlife viewing area, and a visitor center with historical exhibits and convenience store.

lakes.name: Devil's Lake
lakes.description: Devils Lake is a 680-acre lake located located on the central Oregon Coast by Lincoln City. The lake connects to the Pacific Ocean by the D River, once considered the shortest in the world. To the east of the lake is the Siuslaw National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/siuslaw). This lake is appr. 3 miles long and 1/2 mile wide, with an average depth of 10 feet and a maximum depth of 22 feet.

lakes.name: Devils
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5E, Sec 15 and 16 1988:  Located at head of Cayuse Creek in North Fork Willamette River drainage.  No trail access or identifying signs.  Suggested access is to park on Forest Service Road 736 and hike about 3/4 mile south to the lake, using compass and a good Forest Service map; this route climbs a steep hill, then descends into the small basin containing the lake.

lakes.name: Devils Lake
lakes.description: Devils Lake is a beautiful 23-acre shallow lake located adjacent to Century Drive about 30 miles southwest of Bend, Oregon.The exquisite turquoise color of the water coupled with the scenic views makes this lake a delightful place to visit. The easy access, fishable shoreline and stocking of catchable rainbows make Devils Lake an excellent place to take children fishing. In addition to the stocked rainbows there is a natural reproducing population of brook trout. Devils Lake is a shallow lake with an average depth of just over three feet and a maximum depth of 10 feet. Motorized boats are prohibited on this body of water. Small boats or rafts can be launched near the campground. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Dexter Reservoir
lakes.description: Situated 15 miles east of Eugene in the town of Lowell, Dexter Reservoir is the last impoundment on the Middle Fork of the Willamette before it joins the Coast Fork to create the main stem of the Willamette. Steady water levels make Dexter a reliable choice for first-rate boating throughout the year. At 1,025 acres, Dexter is 3.3 miles long and a mile wide at it widest point; it’s regularly stocked with rainbow trout. Dexter State Recreation Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_244.php

lakes.name: Diamond Lake
lakes.description: Diamond Lake is a 2,824-acre lake within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) in southern Oregon, just north of Crater Lake National Park (http://www.nps.gov/crla/index.htm) in Douglas County. This lake is one of the most popular recreational fisheries in southern Oregon. Fishing is done both from boats and from the bank. There are five paved boat ramps, three U.S. Forest Service campgrounds and a privately owned resort around Diamond Lake. Campgrounds: Diamond Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63480), Broken Arrow Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63460), Thielson View Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63618) Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Dinger
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Dog Lake
lakes.description: This is 51- acre reservoir with a 30’ maximum depth. The lake is located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema). The lake contains largemouth bass, yellow perch, crappie, bluegill, brown bullhead, chub, and redband trout. Redband trout must be immediately released. The reservoir has two campgrounds, one on the lake and one about ½ mile from the lake. A boat ramp and pit toilets are available. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Donna
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719.

lakes.name: Dorena Reservoir
lakes.description: This 1,840-acre reservoir is located about six miles east of Cottage Grove, Ore. The reservoir is located in rolling, partially wooded hill country. Its three parks offer boating, swimming, sailing, fishing and water skiing. Schwarz Park, located downstream of the dam, offers overnight camping for families and groups. Overnight camping is also available at Baker Park on the south side of the lake. The lakeshore is managed for wildlife habitat as well, including many species of birds and animals.

lakes.name: Doris Lake
lakes.description: Ninety acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Dorman Pond
lakes.description: An 8-acre pond west of Forest Grove at the junction of Hwy. 8 and Hwy. 6

lakes.name: Double Peaks
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719.

lakes.name: Drews Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 4,517-acre irrigation reservoir about 20 miles west of Lakeview, Oregon. The reservoir contains a small population of redband trout, brown bullhead catfish, and crappie. Lake County maintains a boat ramp with dock and a day-use area with shaded picnic tables. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Duncan Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 33-acre reservoir 5 miles southwest of Silver Lake, Oregon. The reservoir is stocked annually with fingerling, catchable, and trophy rainbow trout. There is an 8-unit campground with pit toilet and a boat ramp. Fishing is good using bait, lures, and files. Fly fishing is better in the morning because this reservoir can be windy in the afternoon. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Dune Lake
lakes.description: Dune Lake is a 2-acre lake located 6.5 miles north of Florence, Ore., on Hwy. 101. There is a trail around the lake that provides ease of access to fishermen.

lakes.name: Dutch Herman Pond
lakes.description: This is a 2-acre pond east of the community of Wolf Creek. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: EE Wilson Pond
lakes.description: A small angling pond located in the EE Wilson Wildlife Area just off Hwy. 99W between Corvallis and Monmouth. The wildlife area is owned and managed by the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife. Address: 29555 Camp Adair Road Monmouth, OR 97361, Ph. (541) 745-5334 Wildlife Area Map (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/maps/wildlife_areas/EEwilson_features.pdf) More information (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/ee_wilson_wildlife_area/index.asp)

lakes.name: East Lake
lakes.description: This is a 1,050-acre lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) appr. 35 miles southwest of Bend. This popular recreation lake is situated in a setting of wooded shorelines, sandy beaches and brilliant blue-green water. The lake is one of two trout lakes nestled in the caldera of the Newberry National Volcanic Monument (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recarea/?recid=66159), the largest ice-age volcano in Oregon. In addition to fishing for kokanee, rainbow trout and brown trout, people come to this lake to picnic and camp. East Lake shares this outstanding geologic area with Paulina Lake and both lakes are well known for their large brown trout. East Lake is known for producing brown trout over 10 pounds. Anglers also like to target rainbow trout. It is a popular fly angling destination East Lake is 6,381 feet above sea level, is approximately a mile in diameter, and the average depth is just over 67 feet. Motorized boats are allowed on this lake and can launch at any of three locations - Cinder Hill Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38324&actid=29), Hot Springs Boating Site (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!?ss=110601&ttype=recarea&recid=38378&actid=78&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Newberry+National+Volcanic+Monument+-+Deschutes+NF+-+Hot+Springs+Boating+Site) and East Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38346&actid=29). Supplies are available at the East Lake Resort and RV Park. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Eastern Brook
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 29 2012:  An 11-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge,  follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on USFS Road 514 to Taylor Burn Campground.  From Taylor Burn Campground,  hike south on the Wahanna Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3583) for about ¼ mile, then take the well-used side trail downhill to the southwest; this trail extends about ¼ mile to Eastern Brook Lake.

lakes.name: Eckman Lake
lakes.description: Thisis a shallow lake about two miles from Waldport, Ore., that drains into the upper Alsea Bay along Hwy 34. The main fish species to be caught are the stocked rainbow trout, native cutthroat trout and the occasional largemouth bass. The shoreline is in private ownership, except for W. B. Nelson State Recreation Area, which provides picnic areas and restrooms on site.

lakes.name: Eddeeleo, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 36 2006:  Hike-in lake, 5 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, take Trail 3553 west and up to the Quinn Lakes Trail junction. Head south on Trail 3597 past Long Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Eddeeleo, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 1 2006:  Hike-in lake, 5 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, take Trail 3553 west and up to the Quinn Lakes Trail junction. Head south on Trail 3597 past Long and Lower Eddeeleo Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Edna
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 29 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897);  turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on USFS Road 514 to Taylor Burn Campground.  From Taylor Burn Campground, USFS Trail 3575 extends from the campground toward Mud Lake and passes within 30 yards of the east shore of Edna Lake (lake is easily visible from the trail).

lakes.name: Eel Lake
lakes.description: This is a 355-acre lake located along Hwy. 101 approximately 13 miles north of North Bend, Ore. It consists of two long narrow arms in the shape of a U. Tugman State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_98.php) is adjacent to the lake and has both a boat ramp and an accessible fishing pier. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Effie Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout. Five acre lake with maximum depth of 14 feet.

lakes.name: Eileen
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R8E, Sec 33 2012:  A 2-acre lake in the Three Sisters Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east; turn right on to Foley Ridge Road (USFS Road 2643) and follow road to the end.  Take USFS Trail 3511 to junction of USFS Trail 3547; continue north on USFS Trail 3547 for approximately 1 mile to reach Eileen Lake.  Eileen Lake is approximately one mile northeast of "The Husband" Mountain” near Linton Meadows.

lakes.name: El
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719.

lakes.name: Elbow
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 13 2012:  A 9-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake to the North Waldo Campground.  From the northeast end of North Waldo Campground, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west. USFS Trail 3590 passes directly around the east shore of Elbow Lake.  Elbow is just south of Chetlo Lake.

lakes.name: Elbow Lake
lakes.description: Elbow Lake is a scenic gem of a lake visible from Hwy. 101. It is easily accessible and is equipped with a small boat ramp. The brushy shoreline makes bank angling difficult but rafts, float tubes and cartop boats are popular. Camping is located nearby at Tahkenitch Lake. Tahkenitch Landing Campground (https://fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42587&actid=42&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Tahkenitch+Landing+Campground) has 36 tent/trailer sites, water, and flush toilets.

lakes.name: Elf
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east to McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242); continue on Hwy 242 to Scott Lake Campground. The easiest access is via USFS Trail 3502 from Scott Lake Campground northwest to Benson Lake (approximately 1-½ miles); from Benson Lake continue, over rough terrain, southwest approximately 400 yards to Elf Lake (preferably with a compass and map).

lakes.name: Elf Lake
lakes.description: Elf Lake is a small, two acre lake. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Elk Lake
lakes.description: This is a 250-acre lake in Deschutes County located approximately 32 miles southwest of Bend, Oregon. This picturesque lake offers sweeping views of Central Oregon's Three Sisters mountains as well as Mt. Bachelor. With depths of 25 to 65 ft., Elk Lake supports populations of kokanee and brook trout and cutthroat trout. Camping and boat ramps are located at Elk Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTSw8jAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAIzTHkw!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!/?ss=110601&ttype=recarea&recid=38860&actid=29&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110130000000000&pnavid=110000000000000&cid=FSE_003705&pname=Central+Oregon+-+Elk+Lake+Campground), Point Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38950&actid=29) and Little Fawn Boating Site.. A fourth boat ramp is available at Elk Lake Resort (http://www.elklakeresort.net/), on the west shore of the lake. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Emigrant Lake
lakes.description: Emigrant Lake is one of the most popular recreational destinations in Jackson County, Oregon. This lake has numerous amenities including developed campgrounds and picnic areas, two boat ramps, restrooms with showers. Reservations are recommended to camp at Emigrant Lake County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3525). Caution: This reservoir is listed under a health advisory for high levels of mercury. The health advisory suggests that anglers only eat trout stocked in the reservoir because they have not had as much time to absorb mercury as fish that spend their whole life in the reservoir. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Emma
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 29 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897);  turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake; continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on USFS Road 514 to Taylor Burn Campground.  From Taylor Burn Campground, hike south on the Wahanna Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3583) for about 1 mile;  Emma Lake is easily visible from the trail, which passes 30 yards to the east.

lakes.name: Empire Lakes
lakes.description: Empire Lakes are two lakes located in northwest Coos Bay, Ore. The lakes are located in John Topits Park, a 120-acre park, which has a boat ramp, day use area, picnic tables, benches, and swimming beach. The lakes are surrounded by forests accessible by more than five miles of mostly paved walking trails. Motorized boats are not permitted on the lakes. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Ercrama
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Bull of the Woods Wilderness. A bushwacking adventure.

lakes.name: Erhart Lake
lakes.description: This 5-acre lake is located about 8 miles south of Florence, Ore. on Hwy 101, adjacent to the west side of the highway. The lake is not marked by signs but is clearly visible through the trees on the west side of the highway. Parking is available at several turnouts along the highway. The shoreline is forested but brushy. The elevation of the lake is near sea level. NOTE: Google Maps incorrectly labels this lake as Loon Lake. However, this is, in fact, Erhart Lake. Campgrounds: Waxmyrtle (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42563&actid=50&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Waxmyrtle+Campground), Lagoon (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42557&actid=31&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Lagoon+Campground), Drftwood II Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42527&actid=31&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Driftwood+II+Campground), Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72DTgBAjAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAObEnGA!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!/?navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&recid=42465&actid=&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&ss=110612&position=&ttype=recarea&pname=Siuslaw%20National%20Forest%20-%20Oregon%20Dunes%20National%20Recreation%20Area). Honeyman State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_134.php). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Erma Bell, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 17 2006:  Hike-in lake, 2.5 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, hike west on the Ollalie Trail 3563 to reach all the Erma Bell.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Erma Bell, Middle
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 17 2006:  Hike-in lake, 2.3 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, hike west on the Ollalie Trail 3563 to reach all the Erma Bell  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Erma Bell, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 20 Hike-in lake, 2 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, hike west on the Ollalie Trail 3563 to reach all the Erma Bell Lakes.

lakes.name: Ernie
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 6 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge,  follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel the east side of Waldo Lake;  from the North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west; after approximately one mile, cut north on USFS Trail 3555 to Kiwa Lake;  from USFS Trail 3555 at the southwest corner of Kiwa Lake,  hike up a steep hill approximately 150 yards to Ernie Lake.

lakes.name: Estacada Lake
lakes.description: This is a 150-acres reservoir on the Clackamas River behind River Mill Dam. Fishing dock and ADA-accessible fishing platform provide the only non-boating access. Boat ramp in Milo McIver State Park at lower end of reservoir, picnic areas, restrooms. Park fee.

lakes.name: Expo Pond
lakes.description: This pond is located at the Jackson County Fairgrounds in Central Point, Ore., just north of the Expo buildings. Easy bank access makes this a good choice for younger anglers. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Fall River
lakes.description: The Fall River is a 12-mile long tributary of the Deschutes River. An ODFW fish hatchery is situated next to the river near its source about 25 miles south of Bend, Oregon and is open from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. Some catchable trout raised at the hatchery are released into the river. The hatchery grounds are open to the public. Limited to fly angling only. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Faraday Lake
lakes.description: Stocked 25-acres reservoir provides year-round fishing. Only bank fishing is allowed -- no boats, rafts, tubes. Located 1.1 miles southeast of Estacada off Hwy 224, the lake is south of the highway next to the Portland General Electric Co. NOTE: PGE is planning work on its hydro facilities at Faraday Lake in 2019  so fishing opportunities will be limited.

lakes.name: Farrell Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout. Small four acre lake with a max depth of 11 feet.

lakes.name: Fat Hippo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5E, Sec 20 (?)

lakes.name: Fawn Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout.  This hike in lake in the Diamond Peak wilderness is 43 acres with a maximum depth of 27 feet.

lakes.name: Fig
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 26 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, travel northeast on Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24) until you reach junction with USFS Road 2422; make a left turn onto USFS Road 2422 and follow this road to junction with USFS Spur Road 269; make a right turn onto USFS Spur Road 269.  Suggested access is to hike about 0.7 mile southeast from the end of USFS Road 269.  Fig Lake is about 2 miles west of Waldo Lake, 600 yards northeast of Cardiac Lake and 200 yards west of Photo Lake.  A compass and good map are helpful.

lakes.name: First
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Fish
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T13S, R7E, Sec 31 2006:  South of Hwy 20 on Hwy 126, just west of  Santiam Junction and 1.5 miles north of Clear Lake. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Fish
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: Not stocked Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Fish Lake Trail #717

lakes.name: Fish Lake
lakes.description: This is a 20-acre lake located in Oregon's high desert about 14 miles southeast of Frenchglen. The lake is in an aspen grove near the rim of Steens Mountain, at an elevation of 7,200 feet. Camping is available nearby at several BLM campgrounds. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Fish Lake - Rogue Watershed
lakes.description: Fish Lake is a 483-acre lake in southern Oregon about 35 miles northeast of Medford. The lake has a maximum depth of 31 feet and an average depth of 18 feet. The lake is located within the Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/rogue-siskiyou/). Camping is available nearby at Fish Lake (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/recarea/?recid=69728), North Fork (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/recarea/?recid=69832) and Doe Point (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/recarea/?recid=69716) campgrounds. Popular activities include swimming, boating, hiking horseback riding, hunting, fishing and wildlife viewing. This is one of only two lakes in Oregon where tiger trout are being stocked on an experimental basis. Fish Lake Resort (http://www.fishlakeresort.net/) offers rental cabins, RV sites, primitive campsites, boat rentals and moorage. Boaters are subject to a 10 m.p.h. speed limit. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Floras Lake
lakes.description: This is a 250-acre lake located about 17 miles south of Bandon, Ore. The lake has a healthy population of cutthroat trout and some largemouth bass. The lake is also stocked with catchable and trophy trout in the spring. Access to the lake is mainly by boat as bank access is limited. This lake reaches a maximum depth of about 50 feet. Boice-Cope County Park (http://www.co.curry.or.us/parks/parks.htm) provides camping and a boat ramp. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Foster Reservoir - Calkins Boat Ramp
lakes.description: This boat ramp is approximately two miles out of Sweet Home on Hwy. 20 Large asphalt parking lot, restroom, and paved boat ramp. Calkins Boat Ramp (http://www.co.linn.or.us/parks/parks/caulkins.html) is maintained by the Linn County Parks Department.

lakes.name: Foster Reservoir - Gedney Cr Boat Ramp
lakes.description: Gedney Creek Park on Foster Reservoir is operated by the Linn County Parks and Recreation Department (http://www.co.linn.or.us/parks/parks/gedneycreek.html). It is located at 43795 North River Drive, on the north shore of the reservoir approximately one mile east of Foster Dam. Open year-round, facilities include a paved boat ramp, dock, and parking spaces for 55 boats.

lakes.name: Foster Reservoir - Sunnyside County Park
lakes.description: This is the largest park on Foster Reservoir east of Sweet Home. Amenities include a boat ramp, boat moorage, RV/tent/group campsites, off-leash dog park, stocked fishing ponds, dump station, showers, picnic shelters, playgrounds, sand vollebyall court More information (http://www.co.linn.or.us/parks/parks/sunnyside.html) Park map (http://www.co.linn.or.us/parks/parks/documents/SunnysideFullPark1.pdf) About Foster Reservoir (http://www.traveloregon.com/Explore-Oregon/Willamette-Valley/Outdoor-Recreation/Water-Sports/Lakes-and-Reservoirs/Foster-Reservoir.aspx)

lakes.name: Found Lake
lakes.description: Nine acre lake, with max depth of 11 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Fourmile Lake
