lakes.description: This is a 740-acre lake located within the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) approximately 40 miles west of Klamath Falls, Oregon. The lake access is west of Lake of the Woods and requires driving down a dirt road for six miles. This lake is at an elevation of 5,800 feet, and the access road is usually open by the Fourth of July, although the lake is open to fishing year-round. Fourmile Lake is a deep, clear lake with a maximum depth of 175 feet. Water levels fluctuate widely as 15,000 acre feet of water is dedicated for irrigating the Rogue Valley. Fourmile Lake is generally stocked with legal- to trophy-sized rainbow trout in late June through August. Lake trout reaching 10 pounds are also available. Kokanee are rare as lake trout have reduced their numbers but average 14 inches. Brook trout are common but difficult to catch. Lake trout, brook trout, and kokanee are all self-sustaining populations. Only rainbow trout are stocked in the lake. Look for caddisfly hatches in July and termite hatches in October. Mosquitoes can be quite bad in the summer. Camping is available near the shoreline at Fourmile Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59727&actid=29), which is located at the foot of Mt. McLaughlin. The campground offers picnic sites, accessible tent and trailer campsites, drinking water, restrooms and an unimproved boat ramp. The Sky Lakes Wilderness (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/recarea/?recid=60187) encompasses most of Fourmile Lake. Several trails lead to high lakes that are stocked every other year with brook trout. The most productive high lake is Badger Lake, which is approximately one mile north of Fourmile Lake. Other lakes within hiking distance that have fish include Squaw, Woodpecker and Long lakes. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Frazier
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Freeway Lake - East
lakes.description: Three ponds located along I-5 about 3 miles south of Albany. One of the ponds is west of the freeway. The other two are east of the freeway. Bank fishing opportunities are good for the two lakes to the east of I-5. Amenities include restrooms and boat ramp for the launching of small boats. Additional, recreational opportunities include paved bike trails, hiking and wildlife viewing. Sarah Helmick State Recreation Site (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_138.php) is nearby.

lakes.name: Frog Lake
lakes.description: This is an 11-acre pond located just off Hwy 26 on the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood) about 8 miles south of Government Camp, Ore. This lake is heavly stocked with both legal- and trophy-sized rainbow troutthroughout the summer. Camping is available on the north end of the lake at Frog Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recarea/?recid=53102). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Fuller Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This small lake is located within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/null/recarea/?recid=63696&actid=93) about 47 miles from Glide, Ore. A lakeside shelter is available. The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Galesville Reservoir
lakes.description: This 630-acre reservoir is open to angling year-round. In addition to trout, the reservoir is sometimes stocked with coho salmon smolts. Many people mistakenly think these fish are kokanee. All of the smolts are adipose fin-clipped. In this reservoir, all landlocked salmon are considered trout and are part of the five-per-day bag limit. This reservoir has a new campground in addition to the picnic area, plus two boat ramps. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Gander
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 34 2012:  A 47-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24); continue east on USFS Road 24 to the junction of USFS Road 2417.  Follow USFS Road 2417 to junction with USFS Road 2417-253 (or USFS Spur Road 253).  Proceed on USFS Road 2417-253 for 1 mile to road’s end and to the USFS Trail 3568 (USFS Trail 3568 ends at junction with USFS Trail 3591).  USFS Trail 3568/USFS Trail 3591 Junction is at the north end of Gander Lake.

lakes.name: Garrison Lake
lakes.description: This is a 134-acre lake located in Port Orford, Ore. It is stocked several times in the spring with trophy and catchable trout. Fishing can be good through May but warmer water and aquatic weeds make for tough fishing through summer. The best way to fish the lake is by boat but bank fishing can be good of the fishing pier on 12th street. Boat ramps are located at 12th Street and Pinehurst Roads. Anglers should keep an eye on the weather and target the lake when winds are light. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Georgia Lake
lakes.description: The two Georgia Lakes are each about 2 acres in size. There are no signs along the highway marking the lakes and parking is available at several small highway turnouts. The lakes are stocked several times each spring with rainbow trout. The shoreline is forested but brushy. The elevation of the lake is near sea level. NOTE: Google Maps incorrectly labels this as Erhart Lake; in fact, this is Georgia Lake.

lakes.name: Gertrude
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R2E, Sec 11.

lakes.name: Gifford
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Lodgepole Trail #706

lakes.name: Gifford, Lower
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Lodgepole Trail #706

lakes.name: Glaze
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east; turn southeast at USFS Paradise Campground and take Old McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242); continue east on Hwy 242 to Scott Lake Campground.  Easiest access is via USFS Trail 3502 northwest 1.5 mile to Benson Lake (from Scott Lake).  Glaze Lake is about 400 yards southeast of Benson Lake and 75 yards northwest of Elf Lake (no trail); strike out over rough terrain preferably with a compass and map.

lakes.name: Gold
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec 30 Popular flyfishing only lake located at end of 2-mile long gravel road leaving Highway 58 just west of the Cascade Mountains summit.  A small Highway Department sign identifies this side road.  A several-unit campground with drinking water and a boat ramp (only electric motors allowed) are at the lake.

lakes.name: Goose
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 9 1988:  In Three Sisters Wilderness, about a mile northeast of Mink Lake and 1/2 mile north of Porky Lake.  Probably no identifying signs and not named on current Forest Service wilderness map.  A developed trail passes along the south shore (lake is easily visible), extending west to Corner Lake and east to Pacific Crest Trail.  A trail from Porky Lake joins this trail near the east end of Goose Lake.

lakes.name: Grace
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 4 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, travel east on Highway 58; take USFS Road 5883 east to USFS Road 5883-381 (Spur Road 381); continue on USFS Spur Road 381 to road’s end.  Grace Lake is located approximate 3 miles northwest of Mt. Fuji.  No trail or easy access; suggested route is to hike downhill a long mile to the north from the end of USFS Spur Road 381 using a compass and good map.  Steep slopes and dense rhododendron growths to the west and north greatly impede access from those directions.  Grace Lake is tucked up close to a steep hillside immediately to the west.

lakes.name: Grass Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Gray Jay Lake
lakes.description: This small,  acre lake is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Green Lakes
lakes.description: Green Lake is a hike-in, alpine lake that spans 83 acres.  It has naturally reproducing brook troutl

lakes.name: Green Peter Reservoir - Thistle Cr. Boat Ramp
lakes.description: Boat ramp on Green Peter Reservoir operated by Linn County Parks & Recreation Department. Amenities include paved ramp and seasonal restrooms. More information (http://www.co.linn.or.us/parks/parks/thistlecreek.html) About Green Peter Reservoir (http://www.nwp.usace.army.mil/include/brochures/GreenPeter_Foster.pdf)

lakes.name: Green Peter Reservoir - Whitcomb Cr. Park
lakes.description: Whitcomb Creek County Park is located on Green Peter Reservoir east of Sweet Home, Oregon. This 328-acre park is managed by the Linn County Parks & Recreation Department. Amenities include basic and group camp sites, boat ramp, group picnic area, bathrooms. About Green Peter Reservoir (http://www.nwp.usace.army.mil/include/brochures/GreenPeter_Foster.pdf)

lakes.name: Hanks Lake, East
lakes.description: Ten acre lake, maximum depth of 22". Stocked with cutthroat trout.

lakes.name: Hanks Lake, Middle
lakes.description: Six acre lake stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Hanks Lake, West
lakes.description: Eight acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Happy
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5E, Sec 27 2012:  An 8-acre lake located in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 two miles east to Kitson Springs Road (USFS Road 23). Turn right, and follow USFS Road 23 to the junction with USFS Road 2149, turn left. Continue on USFS Road 2149 to the trailhead (USFS Trail 3645) on the left; continue on USFS Trail 3645 past Blue Lake to junction of USFS Trail 3653; continue west on USFS Trail 3653 which leads directly to Happy Lake.

lakes.name: Harlequin Lake
lakes.description: Four acre lake, maximum depth of 21 feet, Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Harriet Lake
lakes.description: This is a 23-acre reservoir on the Oak Grove Fork of the Clackamas River in the Mount Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood/). Boat ramp is just past campground. Harriet Lake Campground (http://www.recreation.gov/camping/Lake_Harriet_Or/r/campgroundDetails.do?contractCode=NRSO&parkId=71641&topTabIndex=CampingSpot)

lakes.name: Hartman Pond
lakes.description: Stocked pond is a year-round warmwater and spring trout fishing pond in the Columbia River Gorge. Excellent for non-boating anglers. From I-84, take the Benson State Park exit. The pond is adjacent to the Columbia River adjoining Benson State Recreation Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_147.php).

lakes.name: Harvey
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6.5E, Sec 32 2014:  A 19-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake to North Waldo Campground; from North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of Waldo Lake, continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) to the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on the USFS Road 514 to the Taylor Burn Campground; from Taylor Burn Campground, hike south on the Wahanna Trail (USFS Trail 3583) to Wahanna Lake.  As this trail passes the SW corner of Wahanna Lake it crosses the large and usually dry Wahanna Lake outlet.  After crossing the outlet on the trail, head southwest about 400 yards (bearing 230 degrees), following contours, toward small butte (western shore of Harvey).  If head too sharply west, will run into outlet from Harvey.  There is no trail and lots of downed wood to negotiate through burn.

lakes.name: Haystack Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a large reservoir located in the Crooked River National Grassland (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110601&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110130000000000&pnavid=110000000000000&recid=38274&groupid=null&ttype=recarea&pname=Deschutes) about 9 miles south of Madras, Ore. This is an irrigation reservoir, so water levels can vary substantially seasonally and daily. Haystack Reservoir is open year-round supports a diverse fish population. Legal-sized rainbow trout are stocked in mid-April. Moderate numbers of large brown trout and kokanee are also present, as are several species of warmwater fish. Boat ramps and camping areas are available on the east and west shores. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Head
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Heart
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T14S R6E Sec 3 1988:  In Hackleman Creek Drainage about 4 miles west of Clear Lake.  Reached mainly by a steep and obscure anglers' trail from a Forest Service spur road along the south side of Hackleman Creek.  Developed trails both uphill from this road and downhill from a Browder Ridge  road to the south are reportedly being planned and/or constructed.

lakes.name: Heart Lake
lakes.description: This is a 19-acre lake located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) in a secluded forest setting at an elevation of 5,700 feet. Access is by Forest Road #3715, which is paved but narrow. Boats are allowed on this lake but are restricted to electric motors and subject to a 5 m.p.h. speed limit. Kokanee and rainbow trout are stocked in the lake. Kokanee fishing can be very good in the spring and early summer. Catch rates on rainbow is generally good throughout the summer. Fat head minnow occur in the lake so lures and flies that mimic this food can be effective. Illegally introduced brown bullherad catfish were recently found. There is no limit on catfish; keep as many as you want. This is a day-use only lake. An improved boat ramp, two picnic tables and primitive restrooms are available at the lake. Camping is available nearby at Lofton Reservoir Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59711&actid=29). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Hebo Lake
lakes.description: Hebo Lake is about two acres in size and is fairly shallow. A gravel trail around the lake provides good bank access. There are four ADA barrier-free fishing platforms; two are accessible from the paved road. Only non-motorized boats are permitted on the lake, which is better suited to canoes, small rafts or float tubes. The lake is located near several popular hiking trails. Amenities: Day use area with picnic tables and restrooms, campground with 15 sites. Nearby state parks: Bob Straub State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_183.php), Cape Kiwanda State Natural Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_180.php), Clay Myers State Natural Area (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_248.php). Hebo Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&recid=42705&actid=&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&ss=110612&position=&ttype=recarea&pname=Siuslaw%20National%20Forest%20-%20Hebo%20Lake%20Campground)

lakes.name: Helen
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 28 2012:  A 7-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake to North Waldo Campground; from North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of Waldo Lake, continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) to the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue on to the trailhead at Taylor Butte, about 1 mile east of the Taylor Burn Campground. Helen Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3577) extends from the road (USFS Road 514) to Helen Lake.

lakes.name: Hemlock Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This lake is situated in a secluded moutain setting in the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) at an elevation of 4,000 feet. The lake is approximately 33 feet deep at its deepest point. There is a boat launch and 13 nearby campsites at Hemlock Meadows Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea/?recid=63708). Boats are stricted to electric motors only. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Henry Hagg Lake County Park
lakes.description: This is a 1,110-acre lake seven miles southwest of Forest Grove. Maintained and operated by Washington County, the park features numerous picnic areas, two boat launching facilities, more than 15 miles of hiking trails, and observation decks for wildlife and bird watching. The park, and access to the lake, is now open year-around. Henry Hagg Lake website (https://www.washcoparks.org/scoggins-valley-park)

lakes.name: Hidden
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R5E, Sec 8 1986:  Located about 3 miles south of the south end of Cougar Reservoir.  The lake lies 150 yards west of Forest Service Road 230 and is barely visible from it.  No identifying signs.  A good Forest Service map is necessary to locate Road 230 and the lake's location alongside it.

lakes.name: Hidden Lake
lakes.description: This thirteen acre lake is stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Hidden Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout.  Hidden Lake is 13 acres with a max depth of 21 feet.

lakes.name: Hideaway
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Hideaway Lake Campground

lakes.name: Holbrook Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 40-acre lake located within a quiet forested area of the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema), approximately 37 miles northwest of Lakeview, Oregon. This reservoir is stocked annually with legal-sized and trophy rainbow trout. A boat ramp and a few primitive camping spots are available at this reservoir at Holbrook Reservoir Forest Camp (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/fishing/recarea/?recid=59709&actid=42). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Honey
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T17S, R7E, Sec 15 2012:  A 12-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east past the community of McKenzie Bridge; turn southeast onto Foley Ridge Road (USFS Road 2643). At the end of USFS Road 2643 take Substitute Point Trail (USFS Trail 3511); continue east on USFS Trail 3511 to junction with USFS Trail 3520; continue south on USFS Trail 3520 to junction with USFS Trail 3524; continue southeast on USFS Trail 3524 to Honey Lake.

lakes.name: Horse
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 22 2006:  Upper Horse Lake:  Hike-in lake, 4 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. From Bend, take Hwy 46 (Cascade Lakes Hwy) to the Elk Lake Guard Station. Trail  3517 leads west into the Horse Lakes basin.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Horse, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 21 2006:  Hike-in lake, 5 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. From Bend, take Hwy 46 (Cascade Lakes Hwy) to the Elk Lake Guard Station. Trail  3517 leads west into the Horse Lakes basin.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Horse, Middle
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 22 2006:  Hike-in lake, 4.5 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. From Bend, take Hwy 46 (Cascade Lakes Hwy) to the Elk Lake Guard Station. Trail  3517 leads west into the Horse Lakes basin. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Horsefly
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec 8 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); continue on USFS Road 5897 for approximately 6 miles to the Betty Lake Trailhead.  Betty Lake Trailhead is on the west side of Waldo Lake Road; The hike from the Betty Lake Trailhead on USFS Trail 3664 covers an easy mile, passing Betty Lake and Howkum Lake.  Horsefly Lake sits on the left side of the trail just beyond Howkum Lake.

lakes.name: Horseshoe
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 16 2012:  A 15-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Hwy (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake; continue approximately one-mile south of Elk Lake to the Doris Lake Trailhead; travel west on Doris Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3526) to the Pacific Crest Trail. Continue southwest on the Pacific Crest Trail. Horseshoe Lake is a mile south of Cliff Lake on the east side of the Pacific Crest Trail.

lakes.name: Horseshoe Lake
lakes.description: This is a 14-acre lake located in the Olallie Lake Basin on the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood). There are a few campsites available at Horseshoe Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Horseshoe Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Hosmer Lake
lakes.description: This is a 112-acre lake located about 37 miles southwest of Bend, Oregon. It is well known for its breathtaking views of Mt. Bachelor, South Sisters, and Broken Top, as well as Atlantic salmon, brook trout and rainbow trout. Deep snow is typical during the winter, limiting road access to June through October. Boating is allowed but is restricted to electric motors but not when fishing. Angling is restricted to fly fishing, and float tubes are popular for due to limited backcasting space. Hosmer is a popular canoeing and paddleboarding destination. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Howard Prairie Reservoir
lakes.description: Howard Prairie Lake is a popular destination in sourthern Oregon that offers an abundance of recreational opportunities, including excellent trout and bass fishing. This 1,990-acre reservoir is located about 43 miles south of Medford and 24 miles east of Ashland. It has a maximum depth of 80 feet and an average depth of 35 feet. The lake is formed by the 100-foot tall Howard Prairie Dam, which impounds Beaver Creek, in the Klamath River watershed. Amenities: Asperkaha Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3535), Grizzly County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3536), Klum Landing County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3537), Lily Glen County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3538), Sugar Pine County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3539), Willow Point County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3529), The Resort at Howard Prairie Lake Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Huckleberry
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R4E, Sec 10 2012:  A 6-acre lake in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Westoak Road to High Prairie Road (USFS Road 1928); continue on USFS Road 1928 to USFS Road 1931;  follow USFS Road 1931 for 5 miles to USFS Road 720; continue to junction with USFS Road 725.  From USFS Road 725,  access USFS Trail 3573 for approximately 0.4 mile to Huckleberry Lake.

lakes.name: Huddleston Pond
lakes.description: This is a 5-acre pond located within Huddleston Pond Park in the city of Willamina, Ore. A former mill pond, it contains woody debris that provides habitat for bass and bluegill. It reaches a maximum depth of about 10 feet, with shallow "kid-friendly" edges. It is ADA accessible in places, with a restroom and picnic areas nearby. There is paved parking lot and small ramp for people who want to launch small, non-motorized boats.

lakes.name: Hyatt Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 950-acre reservoir about 13 miles east of Ashland, Ore. There is a large BLM campground (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/site_info.php?siteid=135) with boat ramp at the south end of the reservoir. Boats are restricted to 10 m.p,h. on this lake Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Indian, South
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. A bushwacking adventure.

lakes.name: Indigo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5.5E, Sec 22 2012:  A 17-acre lake located in OR Cascade Recreation Area in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Kitson Springs Road (USFS Road 21); follow USFS Road 21 along the west side of Hills Creek Reservoir; continue on USFS 21 until you reach the junction of USFS Road 2154; follow USFS Road 2154 to the Timpanogas Lake Campground.  A trail begins at the south end of Timpanogas Lake Campground and climbs 700 feet in 1.5 miles directly to Indigo Lake.

lakes.name: Ingo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Willamette National Forest (located just south of Mt. Washington Wilderness).  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east to McKenzie Pass Hwy (Hwy 242); continue on Hwy 242 to Scott Lake Campground. From Scott Lake Campground take USFS Trail 3502 northwest to Benson Lake.  Ingo Lake is 0.8 mile south of Benson Lake and 500 yards east of Valentine Lake. No trail access or identifying sign. Ingo lies in rough, rocky terrain among several other small stocked and unstocked lakes. It can be reached from the south or east, after consulting map and using a compass.

lakes.name: Irish Camp
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R7E, Sec 2 1986:  Located 3/4 mile south of Mt. Washington Wilderness 200 feet north of Forest Service Road 2649 about a mile west of Melakwa Lake.  Easily seen from the road.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Island, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5E, Sec 25 2012:  A 5-acre lake in the Willamette National Forest, a few miles south of Waldo Lake. From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to USFS Road 5883 and follow it to the end.  At the end of USFS Road 5883, is the trailhead for Fuji Mountain and the Island Lakes. Take Trail 3674 east to junction with USFS Trail 3568; continue north on USFS Trail 3568 (Island Lakes are about ½ mile northwest of the junction).  Lower Island Lake is on the south side of the trail, Upper Island is on the north side.

lakes.name: Ivern Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Jefferson County Pond
lakes.description: This is a small pond located in Madras, Oregon, just west of the fairgrounds. Fishermen will find a variety of fish at this pond, including rainbow trout, largemouth bass and crappie. This pond is open only to youth 17 and under and individuals with a disabled angler fishing license. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Jeni
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. A bushwacking adventure from Shining Lake Trail #510.

lakes.name: Jo Ann
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 24 2012:  A 7-acre lake located in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to USFS Road 5883 and follow it to the end. At the end of USFS Road 5883, is the trailhead for Fuji Mountain and the Island Lakes; continue on USFS Trail 3674.  Jo Ann Lake is located approximately 3 miles south of Waldo Lake and 0.6 mile southeast of Lorin Lake.  Both Jo Ann Lake and Lorin Lake can be somewhat difficult to find.  Suggest use of compass and good map.

lakes.name: Johnny Lake
lakes.description: Twenty acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Joyce
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: BLM Molalla River-Table Rock Recreation Area

lakes.name: Junco Lake
lakes.description: Junco is a small, two acre high lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Junction
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 18 2012:  A 21-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  Travel east on Highway 58 to Westfir; continue north on Aufderheider Scenic Byway Road (USFS Road 19) north to Frissel Crossing Campground, at Frissel Crossing Campground take USFS Road 1964; continue east on USFS Road 1964, about 2.5 miles to the short spur (north) accessing USFS Trail 3510; continue on USFS Trail 3510 to junction of McBee Trail (USFS Trail 3523); continue northeast on USFS Trail 3523 to Junction Lake. Mink Lake is approximately ½ mile farther. There are many other trail options

lakes.name: Junction City Pond
lakes.description: This is an 8-acre pond located three miles south of Junction City on the west side of Hwy 99. ODFW stocks the pond regularly with legal and larger rainbow trout making the pond a popular place among anglers.

lakes.name: Juniper Lake
lakes.description: This is a natural lake that is surrounded by both BLM administered land and private land. The lake is stocked biannually with hatchery cutthroat trout. Much of the east shore is administered by the BLM while most he western shore is private land. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Kershaw Lake
lakes.description: Four acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Kidney
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T17S, R7.5E, Sec 15 2012:  A 10-acre lake in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east past the community of McKenzie Bridge; turn southeast onto Foley Ridge Road (USFS Road 2643). At the end of USFS Road 2643 (about 12 miles) take Substitute Point Trail (USFS Trail 3511); continue east on USFS Trail 3511 to junction of USFS Trail 3520; continue south on USFS Trail 3520 to Kidney Lake.  Kidney Lake is ½ mile to the west of Honey Lake.

lakes.name: Kingsley Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 60-acre lake located about 11 miles southwest of Hood River, Oregon. It is also known as Green Point Reservoir. There is a small campground located at the north end of the lake. Boats are allowed. Bank anglers can fish from a gently sloping dam at the north end of the lake as well as other locations. Anglers should focus efforts early in the year, as the reservoir level will drop rapidly as the season progresses. This receiver sometimes receives excess adult hatchery steelhead from Hood River, when available. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Kinnikinnic Lake
lakes.description: Small 2 acre lake stocked with brook trout

lakes.name: Kiwa
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 5 2012:  A 26-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel the east side of Waldo Lake;  From the North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west; after approximately one mile cut north on USFS Trail 3555 to Kiwa Lake

lakes.name: Koko Lake
lakes.description: Koko Lake is a smal three acre lake with max depth of 10 feet.  It is stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Krumbo Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 150-acre reservoir on lower Krumbo Creek in the Malheur Wildlife Refuge (http://www.fws.gov/malheur/) approximately 45 miles south of Burns, Oregon. This reservoir is stocked with catchable rainbow trout. Best fishing is in the spring and fall. There is a picnic area and accessible fishing dock on the soutwest end of the reservoir. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Kuitan
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 11 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From the community of McKenzie Bridge, continue east on Highway 126 past Trail Bridge Reservoir. Approximately 3 miles north of Trail Bridge Reservoir, turn east on Robinson Creek Road (USFS Road 2664).  Continue to the end of USFS Road 2664 where USFS Trail 3508 skirts Robinson Lake and leads on to Kuitan Lake.  Kuitan Lake cannot be seen from the trail and there are no identifying signs.

lakes.name: Lake Billy Chinook
lakes.description: This is a large reservoir about 8 miles southwest of Madras, Oregon created by the impoundment of the Deschutes River behind Round Butte Dam. Lake Billy Chinook is one of Central Oregon's most popular recreational sites. The reservoir supports populations of kokanee, bull trout, and smallmouth and largemouth bass. In addition to fishing, swimming, water skiing and houseboat rentals are popular activities. A boat ramp and camping are available at Cove Palisades State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_32.php). A boat ramp, boat moorage, restaurant, and boat and houseboat rentals are available at Cove Palisades Resort and Marina (http://www.covepalisadesresort.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=12&Itemid=20). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lake Lytle
lakes.description: This is a 65-acre lake located 1/2 mile north of Rockaway Beach on Hwy. 101 The lake sits directly along the east edge of Hwy. 101 at milepost 50. There is a boat ramp off of 12th Street on the north end of the lake.

lakes.name: Lake Marie
lakes.description: This is a 5-acre lake located in the Umpqua Lighthouse State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_121.php) near Winchester Bay, Oregon. It has a sandy beach at one end for swimming and day use, and hiking trails around the perimeter. There is no boat ramp, and motor boats are prohibited. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lake Selmac
lakes.description: Lake Selmac is a 160-acre lake in Josephine County, approximately 25 miles southwest of Grants Pass, Ore. Four campgrounds are located within close proximity of the lake. There is a boat ramp and dock at Lake Selmac County Park (http://www.co.josephine.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=492) on the south shore of the lake. Boats are restricted to a maximum speed of 5 m.p.h. Amenities -- Boat ramp, boat rentals, tent and RV camp sites, yurts, day use picnic areas, fire rings and barbecues, restrooms, vault toilets, trail system, disc golf course, baseball field. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lake in the Woods - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a 4-acre lake located in the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) east of Roseburg, Ore. Motor boats are not allowed. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lake of the Woods
lakes.description: This is a 1,146-acre lake located within the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) between Klamath Falls and Medford, Oregon. This lake is located at an elevation of 4,949 feet above sea level and supports a wide range of recreational activities, including fishing, swimming, water skiing, picnicking, hiking, mountain biking and wildlife viewing. Water quality is very good with depths ranging from an average of 27 to 55 feet. It supports a diverse population of fish. Camping is available at Aspen Point Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59725&actid=29) on the northeast shore of the lake and at Sunset Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110602&ttype=recarea&recid=59731&actid=79&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110400000000000&pnavid=110000000000000&cid=null&pname=Fremont-Winema+National+Forest+-+Sunset+Campground+at+Lake+of+the+Woods) on the west side of the lake. Amenities include piped water, flush toilets, RV dump station, accessible restrooms, boat launch and pier. A day use fee of five dollars is required at all campgrounds and boat ramps. Lodging is also available at Lake of the Woods Lodge, which offers cabins, a nice restaurant, store and gas. For more information, visit the Lake of the Woods website (http://www.lakeofthewoodsresort.com). The most popular fishing is for hatchery rainbow trout in the spring by boat. The most effective method is to troll deep to catch hatchery rainbow trout. Largemouth bass fishing is popular with small tournaments occurring every year. Look for largemouth bass around fallen trees, the many docks that occur in the lake, and around emergent vegetation such as lily pads. Small yellow perch are the most abundant fish in the lake and can be exciting fishing for youth. Small spinners or bait work well for yellow perch. Ice fishing for these fish is popular. A good population of brown bullhead occurs in the lake. A worm or other stink bait fished on the bottom work well for brown bullhead. Look for areas of submergent vegetation. North of Aspen Point Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59725&actid=29) has abundant brown bullhead. Fishing at night is allowed for trout to target the nocturnal brown trout. Trophy brown trout occur in the lake and fishing on the east side of the lake is most productive for brown trout. Try using flourescent colored lures in late evening near the Boy Scout Camp. Hardy anglers can hike into the Mountain Lake Wilderness (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/recarea/?recid=60185) and fish for brook trout and rainbow trout in Weston Lake, Como Lake Harriette, Paragon and Mystic. The Pacific Crest Trail (http://www.fs.usda.gov/pct/) is nearby. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Last
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 26 2012:  An 8-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24), continue on USFS Road 24 to junction; turn left onto Black Creek Road (USFS Road 2421).  From USFS Road 2421, travel ½ mile, turn left onto USFS Road 2422 and follow road’s end. There is a trailhead that begins at the end of USFS Road 2422.  From the trailhead, the shortest access is by forging a path through brush for approximately one mile south and slightly west using a compass and a good map; there is no trail or identifying sign.  Last Lake is 400 yards west of Cardiac Lake and near top of steep cliff dropping south toward Black Creek.

lakes.name: Laurance Lake
lakes.description: This is a 104-acre irrigation reservoir located on the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood) about 23 miles southwest of Hood River, Oregon. Water levels fluctuate with irrigation draws so best to check ahead before heading out to this reservoir. There is a primitive campground and boat ramp at the south end of the reservoir, just off of Laurance Lake Rd. This reservoir is open from late April through October and is stocked with catchable rainbow trout. It also supports a small population of bull trout. Only adipose fin-clipped trout may be retained. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Leaburg Lake
lakes.description: Leaburg Lake is located directly behind Leaburg Dam on the McKenzie River approximately 17 miles east of Springfield on Highway 126 East. The Eugene Water & Electric Board Park (http://eweb.org/recreation) at Leaburg Lake offers 55 acres of grounds and facilities that can accommodate small family picnics as well as large groups of up to 600. Located at milepost 23.5 of the McKenzie Highway, the park offers modern picnic facilities with free electric stoves, softball diamonds, volleyball court, and a horseshoe pit. Leaburg Fish Hatchery (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/leaburg_hatchery.asp)

lakes.name: Lemay
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S R5E Sec 1 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in the Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge continue east on Highway 58 to USFS Road 5884; turn south on USFS Road 5884 and continue to Lake.  Lemay Lake is located in Salt Creek drainage and 300 yards east of USFS Road 5884.   No trail or identifying signs.  Lake cannot be seen from USFS Road 5884, though is visible downhill from a short spur road leading into an old clearcut immediately southwest of the lake.  Easiest access is via an abandoned road extending down to the Lake from USFS Road 5884 just south of its junction with spur road.

lakes.name: Lemish Lake
lakes.description: Relatively shallow lake. Sixteen acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Lemolo Reservoir - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a 415-acre reservoir in the North Umpqua River basin. Three boat ramps, four campgrounds and resort are located on the lake. Lemolo Lake Resort (http://www.lemololakeresort.com/) offers gas, food, lodging, bait, tackle, boat rentals and launching. Spectacular views of the High Cascade Mountains can be seen from the lake. In the winter there is cross-country skiing and a wealth of snowmobile trails that lead through sparkling landscapes. Several Forest Service campgrounds are located in close proximity to the reservoir: Bunker Hill Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63466), East Lemolo Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63492), Inlet Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63508), Poole Creek Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/recarea?recid=63570) Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lils Lake
lakes.description: This small, four acre lake is stocked with cutthroat and rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Lily Lake
lakes.description: Fifteen acre lake with maximum 40' depth. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Linda Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This lake is located within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua/). The trailhead for Calamut Lake begins here. The lake is stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lindick Lake
lakes.description: Eight acre lake with max depth of 25 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Lindy
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S R05E Sec 30 North fork road #1926 to 1927, spur 600 to spur 601; Christy Creek.

lakes.name: Linton
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S R8E Sec 18 2006:  Hike-in lake, 1.5 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Travel east from Springfield on Hwy 126. Past McKenzie Bridge, turn south onto Forest Rd. 242. Just before reaching Alder Springs Campgound, you will find the trailhead on the south side of the highway.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Little Hyatt Reservoir
lakes.description: Description: 7.5 acre surface area reservoir. North part of the reservoir is private property, south part is BLM. Amenities: Primitive site, suitable for personal pontoon boat, or bank fishing. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lizard
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R4E, Sec 12 1988:  At extreme head of North Fork Staley Creek in Middle Fork Willamette River watershed.  No identifying sign.  A 200 yards long unmarked road spur which leaves Forest Service Road 2143 extends down to Liz Lake; this spur allows the lake to be used as a water source for nearby road work or fire suppression.  The lake can't be seen from roads other than this spur.

lakes.name: Lofton Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 42-acre reservoir located in a forest setting at an elevation of 6,100 ft. within the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) approximately 39 miles northwest of Lakeview, Oregon. This reservoir is stocked annually with fingerlings, legal-sized and trophy rainbow trout. Fishing is generally best in the spring. Look for brownrecreation signs on highway 140 directing people to “Lofton Recreation Area”. Itis about 9 miles from highway 140 to Lofton Reservoir. Picnic tables, primitive restrooms, campsites, and primitive restrooms are available on-site at the lake at Lofton Reservoir Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59711&actid=29). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Loleta
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S R3E Sec 15 1988:  In upper end of Coal Creek drainage (Middle Fork Willamette River watershed).  Actually two adjacent lakes, deepened in the 1970s by small earth dams under unauthorized work directed by a Forest Service sale administrator, lying just visible from and downhill about 400 feet north of Forest Service Road 5851.  No identifying signs.

lakes.name: Long
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 25 2006:  Hike-in lake, 3 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, take Trail 3553 west to Trail 3597 south. Long Lake is just past middle Quinn Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Long Lake
lakes.description: Six acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Long Lake
lakes.description: Long Lake is a sixteen acre lake and has a maximum depth of 24 feet.  The lake is stocked with brook trout

lakes.name: Loon Lake
lakes.description: This is a 294-acre lake located in Douglas County about 15 miles southeast of Reedsport, Ore. There are two resorts and a BLM campground (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/site_info.php?siteid=34) with a boat ramp on the north end of the lake. This lake is two miles long and reaches a maximum debth of 105 feet. Boats are allowed and this lake is popular for water skiing. However, it has a speed limit zone for fishing on the south end. It has both BLM and private resort campgrounds and boat ramps. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lopez
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5E, Sec 12 2006:  Hike-in & bushwhack lake, 4.5 miles into Diamond Peak Wilderness in Deschutes National Forest. From Oakridge, take Hwy 58 to Salt Creek Falls. Trail 3662 leads south to Vivian Lake. Follow the lake's outlet stream half a mile down to Lopez. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Loren's Pond
lakes.description: Small pond located about 1/4 mile west of the Trask Fish Hatchery (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/trask_river_hatchery.asp). The pond is located next to the Trask River and a primitive boat ramp giving drift boats access to the lower section of the river, a popular salmon and steelhead fishing area.

lakes.name: Lorin
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5, Sec 24 1986:  Located about 3 miles south of Waldo Lake and 0.6 mile northwest of Joann Lake or Trail 3694 to the south, but lies in no well-defined depression and can be somewhat difficult to find.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Lost Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 3,500-acre impoundment on the Rogue River above Shady Cove, Ore. It is deep throughout, reaching a maximum depth of 322 feet. Camping and a boat ramp is available at Stewart State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_30.php) on the south shore. A second boat ramp is located next to the dam on the north end of the lake. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lost Lake - (Mt. Hood)
lakes.description: This is a 231-acre lake located on the north slope of Mt. Hood within the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood) about 25 miles southwest of Hood River, Oregon. It can be reached from Lost Lake Road (Forest Rd. 13) This lake is open year-round and provides excellent fishing opportunity throught the summer. The picturesque lake also offers excellent boating, although the use of motors is prohibited. Access is generally from when the snow recedes in May through late fall. This lake is stocked with legal- and trophy-sized rainbow trout. It also supports small populations brown and brook trout as well as kokanee. Lost Lake Resort (http://lostlakeresort.org/lost/index.html) at the north end of the lake has a boat ramp, boat rentals. cabins and tent and RV sites. Camping is also available on the east side of the lake at Lost Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=53228&actid=29). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lost Lake - Clatsop County
lakes.description: Lost Lake is located in a forested setting in the Coast Range Mountains west of Hwy. 26 in the Nehalem River basin. The lake is relatively small, about 15 acres. Some camping is available, but no improved facilities exist. The lake offers public bank access around the entire lake. A boat launch is available for small boats. Alternate Activities: Good opportunities for wildlife viewing, particularly deer and elk. The Nehalem River is nearby, offering steelhead fishing in the spring and sea-run cutthroat fishing in the summer. Nearby state parks: Nehalem Bay State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_201.php), Oswald West State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_195.php).

lakes.name: Lost Lake - Douglas County
lakes.description: This is a 6-acre lake east of Hwy 101 mid-way between Tahkenitch and Siltcoos lakes, just south of Florence, Ore. on the Oregon coast. Crown Zellerback Campground is situated next to this lake.

lakes.name: Lost Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Hike in access only

lakes.name: Lower
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Fish Lake Trail #717

lakes.name: Lower Owyhee River
lakes.description: Trout tailrace trout fishery located in Owyhee River downstream of the Owyhee Dam. Catch and release only for brown trout. Rainbow trout stocked in to the river usually in early June. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Lucas
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R5.5E, Sec 21 1989:  Just south of Waldo Wilderness and outer end of USFS Road 5883.  New logging spurs extend south from this road close to Lucas, but we have not determined the easiest route from these spurs to the lake.  No developed trail or identifying sign.

lakes.name: Lucky Lake
lakes.description: Thirty acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Lucky Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 6-acre reservoir located within the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php). The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually. The reservoir is muddy all year and anglers should target areas along the margin of the reservoir to catch fish. Access to the reservoir is through private property with an easement. Please leave gates how you find them. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Mac
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 21 2006:  Hike-in lake, 9 miles into Mink Lake Basin in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 west from Bend to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of the lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail and turn south. Mac is 1.5 miles south of Cliff Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Maidu Lake
lakes.description: This is a 20-acre hike-in lake located approximately 38 miles west of LaPine, Ore., at the head of the North Umpqua River. It is usually accessible by June. It is a 7-mile hike from the Kelsey Valley horse camp side and approximately 3 miles from the Miller Lake side. https://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recreation/hiking/recarea/?recid=63738&actid=51. The lake is annually stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Malheur Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 1,200-acre irrigation reservoir located north of Hwy. 26 between John Day and Ontario, Ore. This reservoir can go dry but is stocked with catchable and fingerling trout during wet cycles, and fishing can be very good during these periods. The reservoir is open year-round but fishing is best in spring and fall. There is a boat ramp on the north shore and bank fishing sports around the reservoir. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Malheur River near Riverside
lakes.description: Malheur River near the old town of Riverside, Oregon is stocked with fingerling-sized rainbow trout in the spring. These trout help to maintain a tailrace trout fishery located from Warm Springs Dam downstream to Juntura. Access is from Juntura on US Hwy 20, approximately 19 miles south on Juntura-Riverside Road. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Mann Lake
lakes.description: This is a natural 200-acre lake located approximately 89 miles southwest of Burns, Oregon. This lake is surrounded mostly by BLM-administered land with some private land along the southeast corner. This lake stocked biannually with hatchery cutthroat trout. It has a two-fish bag limit and a 16-inch minimum size. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Marie No 1
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5.5E, Sec 17 2012:  Travel east on Highway 58 from the City of Oakridge for 2 miles to Kitson Springs County Road. Turn right and proceed on Kitson Springs County Road for 0.5 miles to Forest Road 21. Turn right and follow Forest Road 21 for approximately 29.5 miles, turn left onto Forest Road 2149. Continue about 4.5 miles to Forest Road 2160 and turn right. Proceed about 2 miles to the trailhead. Parking space is on the right side of the road. (USFS website)

lakes.name: Marilyn, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec 31 2006:  Hike-in lake, 1 mile into Willamette National Forest off Hwy 58. From Oakridge, take Hwy 58 to the Gold Lake access road, Forest Rd. 500. The trailhead to the Marilyns is off this road, heading south near the southwest corner of Gold Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Marilyn, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec 31 2006:  Hike-in lake, 1 mile into Willamette National Forest off Hwy 58. From Oakridge, take Hwy 58 to the Gold Lake access road, Forest Rd. 500. The trailhead to the Marilyns is off this road, heading south near the southwest corner of Gold Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Martin Lake
lakes.description: Martin Lake is stocked with rainbow trout. It is a four acre lake with a max depth of 20 feet

lakes.name: Marty
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7.5E, Sec 30 1987:  In Three Sisters Wilderness, 0.6 mile southeast of the top of Condon Butte.  No trail directly to lake.  Is a new homemade, wooden "Marty Lake" sign on tree near south shore.  From a signed trailhead on the Old McKenzie Pass Highway near the Scott Lake access road, hike the trail south and uphill about 2-1/2 miles until reaching a large lava flow along the left (north) side of the trail.  Leave the trail here and follow the lava flow about 0.8 mile north to Marty Lake.

lakes.name: McFarland
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 2 2006:  Hike-in lake, 5 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest near Irish Mt. Take Hwy 19 north from Oakridge. Turn onto Forest Rd. 1958 and take Trail 3307 to the lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: McFarland, East
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 1 1988:  In Three Sisters Wilderness about 0.7 mile east of McFarland Lake.  No access trail or identifying sign.  Easiest access routes are to follow the same elevation contour (using a compass) east from McFarland Lake or to drop southwest about 0.8 mile from the Pacific Crest Trail from a flat but unsigned trail section sometimes call "Cougar Flat."  East McFarland is tucked into a basin surrounded on all sides but north by high rock bluffs.

lakes.name: McKee Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: McKenzie River - lower
lakes.description: Rainbow trout from the Oregon Dept. of Fish and Wildlife's Leaburg Fish hatchery are scatter planted by boat throughout this section of the McKenzie River. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: McKenzie River - upper
lakes.description: Rainbow trout from the Oregon Dept. of Fish and Wildlife's Leaburg Fish Hatchery are scatter-planted by boat throughout this section of the river.

lakes.name: Medco Pond
lakes.description: This a 25-acre pond located between Prospect and Butte Falls northeast of Medford, Oregon. The pond is privately owned by the Swanson Group, an Oregon-based timber company, but is open to the public. The pond is surrounded by pine trees, and behind an earthen dam across the pond is an additional 40 acres of lilly pad water. Amenities are limited, consisting of a couple of port-a-potties and some picnic tables. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Meek Lake
lakes.description: Fourteen acre lake, stocked with brook trout

lakes.name: Melakwa
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R7E, Sec 1 1988:  North of Old McKenzie Pass Highway about 1-1/2 mile southwest of Scott Lake.  From Forest Service Spur Road 686 (access up Scott Creek Road) and unmarked and gated side road extends east about a mile to Melakwa Lake.  Also an old blocked jeep road from the Scott Lake main parking area extends 1-1/2 mile southwest to Melakwa Lake.  A Boy Scout summer camp exists at Melakwa.

lakes.name: Memaloose
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Clackamas Wilderness. Memaloose Lake Trail #515

lakes.name: Mercer Lake
lakes.description: Mercer Lake is a 340--acre lake located about 5 miles north of Florence east of Hwy 101. The lake is open year round. It has 11 miles of shoreline and is accessible both by boat and from the shore. The lake has an average depth of 30 feet deep, with summer temperatures around 70 degrees.

lakes.name: Merle Lake
lakes.description: Nine acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Merrill
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 21 2006:  Hike-in lake, 9 miles into Mink Lake Basin in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 west from Bend to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of the lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail and turn south past Cliff Lake. Merrill is between Horseshoe and Mac Lakes on the Pacific Crest Trail. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Mickey
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 5 1988:  In Waldo Wilderness, 0.4 mile northeast of Lower Rigdon Lake and 200 yards north of Brittany Lake.  No identifying sign.  See Brittany and Dan lakes access descriptions.

lakes.name: Middle Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Midnight Lake
lakes.description: Midnight Lake is stocked with rainbow and brook trout.

lakes.name: Mile
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 22 2006:  Hike-in lake, 5 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. From Bend, take Hwy 46 (Cascade Lakes Hwy) to the Elk Lake Guard Station. Trail  3517 leads west into the Horse Lakes basin. One mile due west of the southern tip of Upper Horse Lake is Mile Lake, then Park Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Mill Flat Reservoir
lakes.description: This is 2-acre pond located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) about 15 miles northwest of Lakeview, Oregon. It can be accessed by the Cox Creek road with passenger vehicles. The reservoir is stocked annually with fingerling rainbow trout that have later been caught in lengths up to 13”. This is great reservoir for people learning to fly fish because there are no trees or bushes to snag on back casts. It does become weedy during the summer, but a small section of open water remains along the dam. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Miller Lake
lakes.description: This is a 600-acre lake located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) approximately 12 miles west of Hwy. 97 and Chemult, Oregon. This lake is stocked annually with rainbow trout but is better known as one of the best brown trout fisheries in Oregon. Brown trout are stocked at 6 inches. Some natural production occurs in the tributaries of the lake. Browns are generally caught by trolling in the deeper water or fishing along the shoreline in the evening. Brown trout fishing is best in November near Evening Creek or near the outlet at Miller Creek. Kokanee are also available but typically are less than eight inches in length and very rare in the catch. Digit Point Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/fremont-winema/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=59715&actid=29) offers a full array of activities, including camping, boating, swimming, fishing and hiking. Amenities include piped water, flush toilets, RV dump station and swimming beach. Abundant populations of small brook trout and brown trout can be found in Miller Creek at the outlet of Miller Lake. This is a great option for youths if fishing is slow in Miller Lake. Bait is allowed. Mosquitoes are very bad in the summer. Miller Lake lamprey were reintroduced into Miller Lake in 2010. ODFW would appreciate any reports of lamprey on fish or circular wounds that might be attributed to lamprey paratism. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Mink
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 17 2006:  Hike-in lake, 7 miles into Mink Lake Basin in Three Sisters Wilderness of the Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, take Hwy 19 north. At Frissel Crossing Campground, head east on Forest Rd. 1964, about 2.5 miles to a short spur that accesses Trail 3510, which leads to the lake. Many other trail options. See Cliff Lake. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Miriam
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 22 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24); continue to end of paved USFS Road 24 (road makes a fork); turn left onto USFS Road 2422 and continue to the Cupit Mary Meadow Trail (USFS Trail 3560); hike north on USFS Trail 3560 to Cupit Mary Meadow.  Miriam Lake is 0.5 mile northwest of the top of Cupit Mary Mountain.  Easiest access is to hike west through a low saddle from Cupit Mary Meadow to Miriam Lake

lakes.name: Mirror
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Salmon-Huckleberry Wilderness. Mirror Lake Trail #664.

lakes.name: Moody
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 16 2006:  Hike-in lake, 9 miles into Mink Lake Basin in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 west from Bend to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of the lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail and turn south. Moody is a mile south of Cliff Lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Moolack
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 24 2006:  Hike-in lake, 2.5 miles into Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Hwy 58 to Forest Rd. 5897, turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake. From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on the old road that meets the Taylor Burn Rd. in about 4 miles. From Taylor Burn Camp, take Trail 3553 west to the Moolack Lake trail, and turn north to the lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Moon Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 619-acre irrigation reservoir located about 16 miles west of Hines, Oregon via U.S. Hwy 20, then about 12 miles south and west to the dam. The last several miles is a rough dirt road. The resevoir is managed for both warmwater fish and trout. Fingerling-sized rainbow trout are stocked in the spring. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Moonlight
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 16 2006:  Hike-in lake, 7 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. From Bend, take Hwy 46 (Cascade Lakes Hwy) to the Elk Lake Guard Station. Trail  3517 leads west into the Horse Lakes basin. Past Horse Lake, follow Trail 3514 (Horse Creek Trail) to Moonlight.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Mosquito, Little
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5E, Sec 27 1988:  In upper Middle Fork Willamette River drainage, 1/4 mile east of Forest Service Road 2154.  New logging road spurs approach closed to the lake but it can still be difficult to find; here is one suggested route:  On Road 2154, park at the second wide spot downhill from Spur 383 and hike uphill about 500 yards to the northeast; the lake lies down a steep slope just over the first major rhododendron-covered ridge.  No access trails or identifying signs.  This is not the other lake about a mile southeast in Section 26 identified as Mosquito Lake on some Forest Service maps.

lakes.name: Mt. Hood Pond
lakes.description: This is a 5-acre pond on the campus of Mt. Hood Community College in Gresham. Angling is restricted to youths age 17 and under and holders of ODFW's Disabled Anglers permits from April 1 - Aug. 31.

lakes.name: Mud Lake
lakes.description: This is a 168-acre irrigation reservoir located within the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php). The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually. Sampling in 2012 found rainbow trout up to 12”. This is a great location to base out of and explore the desert, Hart Mountain National Antelope Refuge (http://www.fws.gov/sheldonhartmtn/Hart/index.html), or a cast and blast weekend for quail or chucker. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Mud No 2
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 20 1988:  In Waldo Wilderness, about 3/4 mile north of Taylor Burn Campground.  Two trails leave the campground area, passing near the east and west shores of the lake; which to take is a toss-up.  The lake can be seen, if watching closely, from either trail.  Probably no identifying signs.

lakes.name: Munsel Lake
lakes.description: Munsel Lake is a 104-acre lake located about 1.5 miles north of Florence, Ore., east of Hwy 101. This lake ranges in depth from 4 to 10 ft. and serves as an important recreational use area for boating, swimming and fishing.

lakes.name: Murphy Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a small irrigation reservoir located in narrow steep sided draw north and east of Beulah Reservoir near Juntura, Ore.. Bank access is limited to the dam area. Road is a narrow rough two track off the Beualh- Becker Horse Camp road. Road access is limted in spring when soft. Stocked with fingerling rainbow trout in late spring. In years with low conditions this reservoir may not be stocked. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Nash
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T17S, R7E, Sec 34 2006:  Hike-in lake, 6 miles into Sisters Mirror Lake Basin in Three Sisters Wilderness of Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 from Bend to Elk Lake Resort. Pass the turn-off to the resort and continue north 2.5 miles to the trailhead, less than half a mile north of Sink Creek. Take Trail 20 to the Sisters Mirror Lakes, then continue west to Nash.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Nedonna Pond
lakes.description: This is a small pond located just north of Rockaway on Oregon's north coast. The pond is a short distance off Hwy 101 in the Manhattan Beach area, on the right just after crossing the railroad tracks.

lakes.name: Nekbobets
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719 or PCT Trail #2000.

lakes.name: Nornie
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 1988:  Just outside south boundary of Mt. Washington Wilderness, one mile southwest of Benson Lake, about 200 yards north of Valentine Lake, and 300 yards southeast of Island Lake.  A small unnamed lake lies between Nornie and Howie Lake, 1/4 mile to the north.  No trail access or identifying sign.  Use a good map or aerial photo, and take compass course from a known point.

lakes.name: North Fork Reservoir - Promotory Park
lakes.description: Promotory Park is the largest and most developed of three sites on North Fork Reservoir where ODFW releases rainbow trout.This park has a boat ramp, boat moorage, 50 campsites, picnic areas, electric cooking facilities, boat rentals, grocery store, and ADA-accessible fishing platform. Youth fishing: Just a few hundred yards from North Fork Reservoir at Promotory Campground is Small Fry Lake. This one-acre lake was developed for the exclusive use of young anglers. Kids up to 17 years old can fish for their limit of two fish per day.

lakes.name: North Fork Reservoir Site #1
lakes.description: This is a 350-acre reservoir of the Clackamas River behind North Fork Dam approximately 5.2 miles east of Estacada, Ore.. This stocking site is sourth of Hwy. 224 and has an ADA accessible fishing dock.

lakes.name: North Fork Reservoir Site #2
lakes.description: This site is located at the confluence of the North Fork Clackamas River and North Fork Reservoir. It is a small turnout and dirt parking lot north of Hwy 224.

lakes.name: North Georgia Lake
lakes.description: The two Georgia Lakes are each about 2 acres in size. There are no signs along the highway marking the lakes and parking is available at several small highway turnouts. The lakes are stocked several times each spring with rainbow trout. The shoreline is forested but brushy. The elevation of the lake is near sea level. Note: Google Maps shows this as Georgia Lake; in fact, this is North Georgia Lake. Campgrounds: Waxmyrtle (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42563&actid=50&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Waxmyrtle+Campground), Lagoon (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42557&actid=31&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Oregon+Dunes+National+Recreation+Area+-+Lagoon+Campground), Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72DTgBAjAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAObEnGA!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!/?navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&recid=42465&actid=&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&ss=110612&position=&ttype=recarea&pname=Siuslaw%20National%20Forest%20-%20Oregon%20Dunes%20National%20Recreation%20Area), Honeyman State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_134.php)

lakes.name: North Lake
lakes.description: North Lake is a half-acre lake located within the Siuslaw National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/siuslaw) that lies on the ridgeline above Hebo Lake. Nearby South Lake has a U.S. Forest Service campground that is open year around. The last several miles of road to the lake are unimproved forest road and rough; use caution. It is not advisable to take trailers to this lake.

lakes.name: North Matthieu Lake
lakes.description: North Matthieu Lake is stocked with brook trout. It is a six acre lake with a max depth of 15 feet.

lakes.name: North Santiam River - Bridge at Bruno Mtn Rd
North Santiam River - Bugaboo Creek Bridge
North Santiam River - Cooper's Ridge Road
lakes.description: The North Santiam River is one of just a few Oregon rivers where trout stocking takes place. on the North Santiam, fish are released at several sites.

lakes.name: North Santiam River - Downing Creek
lakes.description: The North Santiam River is one of just a few Oregon rivers where trout stocking takes place. on the North Santiam, fish are released at several sites.

lakes.name: North Santiam River - Idanha Bridge
lakes.description: The North Santiam River is one of just a few Oregon rivers where trout stocking takes place. on the North Santiam, fish are released at several sites.

lakes.name: North Santiam River - Riverside Campground
lakes.description: The North Santiam River is one of just a few Oregon rivers where trout stocking takes place. on the North Santiam, fish are released at several sites.

lakes.name: North Santiam River - Straight Creek Rd. Bridge
lakes.description: The North Santiam River is one of just a few Oregon rivers where trout stocking takes place. on the North Santiam, fish are released at several sites.

lakes.name: Notch
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5E, Sec 14 2012:  A 2-acre, hike-in lake in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge continue east, exit towards Hills Creek Reservoir; stay to the left accessing Hills Creek Road #23; follow Road 23 for 20 miles till you arrive at the Vivian Lake trailhead; the route from the trailhead heads northeast for eight-tenths of a mile till it reaches Notch Lake.

lakes.name: Ochoco Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 1,100-acre reservoir located approximately 5 miles east of Prineville, Oregon on Hwy 26. This reservoir is open year-around and is stocked with sub-legal rainbow trout, which later reach sizes of 12-15 inches. There are also wild redband trout available from natural production in tributary streams. Black crappie fishing is excellent during the summer months. Boats are allowed on this reservoir and can launch from a ramp at Ochoco Lake Campground (http://www.ccprd.org/OchocoLake.php), which has 22 self-contained camping spaces available April 1 through spproximately October 31 each year, depending on weather. Facilities include picnic tables, fire pits, restrooms and fish cleaning station. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Odell Lake
lakes.description: This is a 3,300-acre natural mountain lake located on the east side of Willamette Pass approximately 70 miles southeast of Eugene, Oregon just off Hwy 58. Fed by a numerous small creeks and springs, this lake supports good populations of kokanee, lake trout and redband trout.The lake also supports a small population of bull trout, which are protected and must be released unharmed. There are several improved boat ramps around the lake, and trolling with downriggers, leaded line and flashers are popular fishing techniques, as is casting from shore. Kokanee are abundant and average 11-13 inches while lake trout over 10 pounds are common. Camping is available at Princess Creek (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39088&actid=29), Trapper Creek (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39120&actid=29) and Sunset Cove (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=39112&actid=29) campgrounds, which are managed by the U.S. Forest Service. Overnight accomodations are available Shelter Cove Resort (http://www.sheltercoveresort.com/) and Odell Lake Lodge and Resort (http://odelllakeresort.com/), which offer cabin rentals, supplies, boats and tackle. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Olalla Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: Olalla Creek Reservoir is a 120-acre reservoir on Olalla Creek about a mile north of Toledo, Ore. The reservoir reaches a maximum depth of about 65 feet near Olalla dam. The shoreline and much of the surrounding landscape is owned by Georgia Pacific Corporation and managed as commercial timberland. The company allows public access to the water, and small boats without motors may be launched.

lakes.name: Olallie Lake
lakes.description: This is the largest of more than 200 lakes within the Olallie Lake Scenic Area. Located on the southern edge of the Mt. Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood) it is a popular summer recreational destination for people from Portland and Salem, Ore. There are three campgrounds and a rustic cabin resort on this lake as well as a hiking trail that encircles the perimeter. Yurts, cabins, and boat rentals are available at Olallie Lake Resort (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=52978&actid=29). There is a boat ramp at Peninsula Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=52994&actid=29) on the southwest shore of the lake. Camping is also available at Olallie Meadows Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=52982&actid=29) and Paul Dennis Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=52990&actid=29). Olallie Lake is also a popular jumping off point for backpackers who want to fish the surrounding high lakes or access the Pacific Crest Trail (http://www.fs.usda.gov/pct/). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Oldenberg Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout. Twenty eight acre lake with a maximum depth of 29 feet.

lakes.name: Opal
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5.5E, Sec 9 2006:  In Willamette National Forest just west of the southern boundary of Diamond Peak Wilderness. From Oakridge take Hwy 58 to Crescent Junction. At the southwest end of Crescent Lake, take Forest Rd 211 west, then Forest Rd. 398 south to Opal Lake. Timpanagos Lake is farther down this road.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Otter
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 8 2012:  A 14-acre lake in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  Travel east on Highway 58 to Westfir; proceed north on Aufderheider Scenic Byway (USFS Road 19) to USFS Road 1957 (Box Canyon Road); follow USFS Road 1957 to Skookum Creek Campground.  From Skookum Creek Campground head south on USFS Trail 3563 for 0.3 mile; turn left on USFS Trail 3589; which takes you to Otter Lake.

lakes.name: Overton Pond
lakes.description: This small reservoir is located on the Fremont-Wnema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) about 20 miles northeast of Lakeview. This reservoir can be accessed using Forest Service Road #3615 then turning onto Forest Service Road #3624 when the pavement ends (about 15 miles off of Hwy 140).
