The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually and they have been caught to 12”. Milfoil covers the pond from June to September but a hole can open when the wind blows from the right direction. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Paddock
lakes.description: AccessDirections: 24S, R5E, Sec 24 2012:  A 2-acre lake located in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Crescent Lake Junction; drive around the northwest shore of Crescent Lake on USFS Road 60 to USFS Road 6010.  Continue west on USFS Road 6010 to Summit Lake Campground; From Summit Campground continue on USFS Road 380 west to Trailhead (north side of road) for USFS Trail 3699;  on USFS Trail 3699,  hike north for .25 mile and then cut west across country to Ruth Lake. From Ruth Lake, Paddock Lake is 0.3 mile west and 150 yards southwest of Neet Lake.  Suggest use of good map and compass.

lakes.name: Paiute Reservoir
lakes.description: This is an irrigation reservoir located about 20 miles east of Adel, Oregon approximately one mile south of Hwy 140. The road to access the reservoir is located between milepost 49 and 50 and has a stop sign. Some years this reservoir is full while other years it is dry. The reservoir is stocked with rainbow trout and Lahontan cutthroat trout when there is water. The water is muddy and anglers should target the margins for better success. Ice fishing is available at this reservoir some years. Call the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview Field Office (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/notesdirectory.php) to verify that water is in the reservoir. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Patsy Lake
lakes.description: Patsy Lake is stocked with rainbow trout.  It is a small 2 acrea, high elevation lake.

lakes.name: Paulina Lake
lakes.description: This is a 1,520-acre lake located within the Newberry Volcanic National Monument (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recarea/?recid=66159), about 25 miles south of Bend, Oregon. The lake is situated in the caldera of the Newberry volcano at an elevation of 6,350 ft. It has a maximum depth of 250 ft. and is open to motorized boats, which can launch from ramps at Little Crater Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38400&actid=29) and Paulina Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=38442&actid=29). The Paulina Lake Lodge offers lodging, meals and supplies. Paulina provides great opportunity for brown trout, rainbow trout and kokanee. The state record brown trout was caught here. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Pear Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Penn
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 8 2006:  Hike-in lake, 9 miles into Mink Lake Basin in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 west from Bend to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of the lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail and turn south. Turn west again onto the Goose/Corner Lakes Trail. Penn Lake is one quarter mile northwest of Corner.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Perkins Lake
lakes.description: PerkPerkins Lake is a 5-acre lake located in the Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area south of Florence. Fish Fish species: rainbow trout OD

lakes.name: Photo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 25 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, travel northeast on Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24) until you reach junction; make a left turn onto USFS Road 2422 and follow this road to next junction; make a right turn onto USFS Spur Road 269.  Suggested access is to hike about 0.7 mile southeast from the end of Forest Service Road 269.  Photo Lake is about 2 miles west of Waldo Lake, 600 yards northeast of Cardiac Lake and 200 yards east of Fig Lake.  A compass and good map are helpful.

lakes.name: Pine Hollow Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 240-acre irrigation reservoir located approximately 39 miles south of The Dalles, Oregon. The lake is surrounded by private homes but fishing access has been preserved through a 10-foot easment around the reservoir purchased by ODFW, although public bank fishing is limited to the area west of the south shore boat ramp and north and south of the east shore ramp. There are two public boat ramps and a privately operated resort on the east shore. Fishing is allowed year-round, although the lake sometimes freezes over during the winter. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Plat I Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 155-acre reservoir on Sutherlin Creek in Douglas County, Oregon. It is owned by Sutherlin Creek Water Control District and is open to fishing year-round. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Plaza
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Salmon-Huckleberry Wilderness. Plaza Lake Trail #788

lakes.name: Poison Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a small BLM irrigation reservoir located about 38 miles north of Burns, Oregon within the Malheur National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/malheur). It is stocked in the spring with fingerling-sized rainbow trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Pole Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 200-acre irrigation reservoir located 25 miles north of Vale, Oregon via US Hwy 26. The reservoir sometimes dries up but is stocked in the spring with both fingerling-sized and catchable rainbow trout. The reservoir is located on private land so it is day-use only with no overnight camping. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Porky
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 17 2006:  Hike-in lake, 8 miles into Mink Lake Basin in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 46 west from Bend to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of the lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail and turn south. At Cliff Lake, take the trail east to Porky. 3/4 mile northeast of Mink Lake.  Popular trails access the lake from four directions.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Powers Pond
lakes.description: This is a 23-acre former mill pond located in Powers Park next to the Coquille River in the southwest Oregon town of Powers. The park has RV facilities, showers and a picnic area. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Priday Reservoir
lakes.description: This 210-acre irrigationreservoir is located 5 miles south of Plush, Oregon in the Warner Basin on thewest side of the Plush-Adel road. The reservoir is turbid (muddy) most of thetime and most fish are caught close to shore. Brown bullhead catfish and a fewcrappie are available also. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Prince
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R7E, Sec 3 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From the community of McKenzie Bridge, continue to Frissel Boat Landing; at Frissel Boat Landing turn right (east); continue on USFS Road 2649 (Scott Creek Rd); at the three way junction (USFS Rd 2653 to left, USFS Rd 2649 middle road-straight ahead, and USFS 2649 Road(loop) to right), take the middle road straight ahead (USFS Road 2649);  from USFS Road 2649 (middle road), continue east to USFS Spur Road 676 and Fingerboard Prairie Trailhead (USFS Trail 3508). Continue north on USFS Trail 3508 and as the trail makes a turn to the west,  Prince Lake will be on the south side of the trail.

lakes.name: Prineville Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 3,000-acre reservoir located approximately 17 miles southeast of Prineville. This reservoir was created for irrigation and flood control by damming the Crooked River behind Arthur R. Bowman Dam. It is situated at an elevation of 3,200 ft. and reaches depths of up to 120 ft. The reservoir is open to motorized boats and supports a diverse population of fish, including rainbow trout, black crappie and smallmouth and largemouth bass. The reservoir is stocked annually with sublegal rainbow trout. A boat ramp, boot moorage, picnic areas are available at Prineville Reservoir State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_34.php). Lodging and supplies are available at the Prineville Reservoir Resort. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Prineville Youth Pond
lakes.description: Prineville Youth Pond is a small pond located in Prineville, Oregon next to Crook County Christian School and across the street from Crook County Fairgrounds. This pond is stocked every spring and fall with catchable rainbow trout. Fishing is restricted to youths 17 and under. ODFW sometimes hosts family fishing events at this site. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Progress Lake
lakes.description: This is a 10-acre pond next to the Progress Ridge Town Center in Beaverton, Oregon. The pond is an old rock pit and has a maximum depth of 54 feet. There is a sidewalk, fishing platform and viewing platform on one side of the lake. The lake is owned by Tualatin Hills Parks and Rec (http://www.thprd.org/). Boating and swimming are prohibited on this lake.

lakes.name: Puppy Lake
lakes.description: Eight acre lake stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Pyramid
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Pyramid Lake Trail #727

lakes.name: Quartzville Creek - Boulder Cr. Bridge
lakes.description: Quartzville Creek is a wild and scenic river extending 12 miles upstream from Green Peter Reservoir to the Willamette National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/willamette) boundry. It offers a variety of recreational opportunities such as gold panning, fishing, camping and high-flow kayaking. The Quartzville Back Country Byway (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/files/brochures/quartzville_110119.pdf) parallels the winding river, offering views of old-growth forests and wildlife and providing easy access to developed and undeveloped picnic and camping sites.

lakes.name: Quartzville Creek - Concrete Bridge
lakes.description: Quartzville Creek is a wild and scenic river extending 12 miles upstream from Green Peter Reservoir to the Willamette National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/willamette) boundry. It offers a variety of recreational opportunities such as gold panning, fishing, camping and high-flow kayaking. The Quartzville Back Country Byway (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/files/brochures/quartzville_110119.pdf) parallels the winding river, offering views of old-growth forests and wildlife and providing easy access to developed and undeveloped picnic and camping sites.

lakes.name: Quartzville Creek - Old Miner's Meadow
lakes.description: Quartzville Creek is a wild and scenic river extending 12 miles upstream from Green Peter Reservoir to the Willamette National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/willamette) boundry. It offers a variety of recreational opportunities such as gold panning, fishing, camping and high-flow kayaking. The Quartzville Back Country Byway (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/files/brochures/quartzville_110119.pdf) parallels the winding river, offering views of old-growth forests and wildlife and providing easy access to developed and undeveloped picnic and camping sites.

lakes.name: Quartzville Creek - Packers Cr. Bridge
lakes.description: Quartzville Creek is a wild and scenic river extending 12 miles upstream from Green Peter Reservoir to the Willamette National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/willamette) boundry. It offers a variety of recreational opportunities such as gold panning, fishing, camping and high-flow kayaking. The Quartzville Back Country Byway (http://www.blm.gov/or/resources/recreation/files/brochures/quartzville_110119.pdf) parallels the winding river, offering views of old-growth forests and wildlife and providing easy access to developed and undeveloped picnic and camping sites.

lakes.name: Questionmark
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 10 2012:  A 6-acre lake in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Hwy (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake; from the south end of Elk Lake take the Doris Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3526); continue west on USFS Trail 3526 (USFS Trail 3526 will “zigzag” across the Pacific Crest Trail and continue on) to Vera Lake; continue on USFS Trail 3526 past Vera Lake (south side of trail) approximately 200 feet to Questionmark Lake (north side of trail).

lakes.name: Quinn, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 24 2012:  A 12-acre lake in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake; continue north on old road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514) to Taylor Burn Campground.  From Taylor Burn Campground, take USFS Trail 3553 west to USFS Trail 3597 turn south. Lower Quinn is about a mile; Upper Quinn a quarter mile farther south.

lakes.name: Quinn, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R5.5E, Sec 25 2012:  .  A 15-acre lake in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake; continue north on old road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514) to Taylor Burn Campground.  From Taylor Burn Campground, take USFS Trail 3553 west to USFS Trail 3597.  USFS Trail 3597 goes south and in about 1.25 miles passes within 300 yards of the east side of Upper Quinn Lake. Upper Quinn can't be seen from this trail, but two well-defined side trails extend to the lake.

lakes.name: ROK
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 22 2012:  A 1-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24); continue to end of paved USFS Road 24 (road makes a fork); turn left onto USFS Road 2422 and continue to the Cupit Mary Meadow Trail (USFS Trail 3560); hike north on USFS Trail 3560 to Cupit Mary Meadow.  Access is by hiking uphill ¼ mile to the west from USFS Trail 356.  ROK Lake is 0.3 mile southeast of Miriam Lake.

lakes.name: Rae
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R3E, Sec 13 1988:  In Middle Fork Willamette River (Buck Cr) drainage.  Immediately adjacent to south shoulder of Forest Service Road 2129 in extreme southeast corner of Section 13.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Raft Lake
lakes.description: Ten acre lake (30' ft max depth), stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Red
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Red Lake Trail #719

lakes.name: Red Top Pond - Umpqua
lakes.description: This couple acre pond is situated near the headwaters of Deadman Creek in the Umpqua River basin. Fishing access includes bank, float tube, inflatable pontoon boat, and canoes. There is dispersed camping near the lake. The area around the lake is owned by the Cow Creek Tribe, please be respectful of the land. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Reinhart Pond
lakes.description: This pond is located within Reinhart Volunteer Park (http://www.grantspassoregon.gov/Index.aspx?page=158) at 1690 SW Webster Rd. in Grants Pass, Ore. Amenities at this riverside park include softball and baseball fields, a soccer field, sand vollehyball courts, flycasting pond, jogging trail, tennis and basketball courts, and shelters available for reservations. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Rigdon, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 5 2012:  A 20-acre lake located in Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel the east side of Waldo Lake; from North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of Lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west; after approximately one mile cut north on USFS Trail 3555 to Lower Rigdon Lake.  Lower Rigdon Lake is one mile north of Waldo Lake and 1/4 mile north of Upper Rigdon Lake. The trail directly skirts the east shores of both Rigdon Lakes.

lakes.name: Rigdon, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 8 2012:  A 29-acre lake located in Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel the east side of Waldo Lake; from North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of Waldo Lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west; after approximately one mile cut north on USFS Trail 3555 to Lower Rigdon Lake.  Upper Rigdon Lake is one mile north of Waldo Lake and 1/4 mile south of Lower Rigdon Lake. The trail directly skirts the east shores of both Rigdon Lakes.

lakes.name: Rimrock
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Cross country from Red Lakes Trail #719 or PCT Trail #2000.

lakes.name: Roaring River Park Pond
lakes.description: A small pond in a day-use park near ODFW's Roaring River Fish Hatchery. Roaring River is one of ODFW's primary trout rearing facilities. It has an outdoor display board, fish viewing pond. Not currently stocked More information (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/visitors/roaring_river_hatchery.asp) Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Robinson
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 3 2012:  An 8-acre lake located in Mt. Washington Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From the community of McKenzie Bridge, continue east on Highway 126 past Trail Bridge Reservoir, turn east on Robinson Creek Road (USFS 2664).  Continue to the end of Robinson Creek Road (USFS 2664), a trail (USFS Trail 3508) extends from trailhead about ½ mile to Robinson Lake.

lakes.name: Rock Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 100-acre pond located within the Mt. Hood Forest approximately 43 miles southwest of The Dalles, Oregon. This reservoir is located in a scenic forested area and is subject to irrigation drawdowns in the summer. It is open to boats, and trolling can be an effective fishing tactic, although the lake is shallow and has plenty of bank access as well. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Rock Rim Lake
lakes.description: Four acrea lake stocked with brook trout

lakes.name: Rock, Lower
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Serene Lake Trail #512

lakes.name: Rock, Middle
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Serene Lake Trail #512

lakes.name: Rock, Upper
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Serene Lake Trail #512

lakes.name: Rockpile
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5.5E, Sec 17 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Crescent Lake Junction; drive around the northwest shore of Crescent Lake on USFS Road 60 to Summit Lake Road (USFS Road 6010).  Where the road crosses the Pacific Crest Trail (west of Summit Lake Campground) take the Pacific Crest Trail north.  The Pacific Crest Trail intersects with USFS Trail 3662 in about 2 miles.  Turn west on USFS Trail 3662 and continue for ¼ mile.   Rockpile Lake is a few hundred yards to the south (Marie Lake is about ½ mile farther).

lakes.name: Rogue River above Lost Creek
lakes.description: This beautiful section of the Rogue River is stocked with rainbow trout regularly throughout the summer. Much of this section of the river is within the Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/rogue-siskiyou/). A maintained trail follows the entire length of this section of the river, and U.S. Forest Service campgrounds and day-use areas are available Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Rosary Lake, Lower
lakes.description: Lower Rosary Lake has naturally reproducing brook trout. It spans  32 acres.

lakes.name: Rosary Lake, Middle
lakes.description: Middle Rosary Lake is stocked with rainbow trout. It is nine acres with a max depth of 31 feet.

lakes.name: Round
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 1 2012:  A 24-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, take the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) west.  Continue on USFS Trail 3590 to junction with USFS Trail 3597; follow USFS Trail 3597 north to Round Lake.    USFS Trail 3597 passes around the south and west shore, 500 feet from Round Lake; easily visible, downhill from the trail.

lakes.name: Round
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: Not stocked Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. About 1/4 mile bushwack from NF Rd 6370.

lakes.name: Round Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: Ruth No 1
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5E, Sec 24 1989:  In south end of Diamond Peak Wilderness, 0.3 mile north of the old wagon road (Forest Service Road 380).  A signed trailhead exists on this road and leads to (around the west shore) and past Ruth Lake.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Ruth No 2
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 22 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the Lake, take old road (USFS Road 516) (north) to Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514) junction; at junction follow Jackpine Way Trail (USFS Trail 3587) north; hike 2 miles to Lo Lake.  Jackpine Way Trail (USFS Trail 3587) passes immediately along the northeast shore of Lo Lake.  Ruth Lake No. 2 is just out-of sight, 30 yards south of Lo Lake.

lakes.name: Salish Pond -West
lakes.description: One of two ponds located in the 70-acre Salish Ponds Wetlands Park in Fairview, Ore. The 6-acre west pond is regularly stocked with rainbow trout. It also contains warmwater fish. The east pond is stocked periodically with warmater fish transplanted by the Oregon Bass and Panfish Club. Boats and swimming are not allowed. Facilities: restrooms, nature trail, disabled accessible docks at both ponds, nearby stores that supply fishing gear.

lakes.name: Salmon, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 10 2012:  A 7-acre lake in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24); continue on USFS Road 24 until it becomes USFS Road 2421; turn left (north) on USFS Road 2422 and continue to trailhead (past Spirit Lake); from trailhead take USFS Trail 3591, which leads to the west shore of Upper Salmon Lake.  From Upper Salmon Lake, hike west ¼ mile on flat terrain to Lower Salmon Lake.

lakes.name: Salmon, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 10 1982:  In Waldo Wilderness, 1/4 mile east of Lower Salmon Lake.  Trail 3591 (between Spirit Lake and Waldo Meadow) passes close to the lake's west shore.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Salmonberry Lake
lakes.description: Salmonberry Lake is owned by the city of St. Helens and is about three acres in size. It is a former municipal water supply secluded in the woods off of Pittsburgh Road. The road to the pond is gated and anglers must walk about 1/3 mile to access this pond.

lakes.name: Sapphire
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 13 2012:  A 5-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to Salmon Creek Road (USFS Road 24); continue on USFS Road 24 (until it becomes USFS Road 2421); turn left (north) on USFS Road 2422; follow USFS Road 2422 to end and to trailhead; follow USFS Trail 3576 east for approximately 1 mile to Waldo Lake; from Waldo Lake head north on the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) to Elbow Lake; continue past Elbow Lake.  Suggested route is to hike cross-county for ½ mile southwest from Elbow Lake (Zircon Lake is 500 yards east of Sapphire Lake). Recommend access with good map and compass.

lakes.name: Saunders Lake
lakes.description: This is a 55-acre lake west of Hwy. 101 about 15 miles south of Reedsport, Ore. There is a county park with a picnic area, boat ramp and bank fishing at the south end of the lake. The rest of the lake shore is residential land and no camping is allowed. However, campgrounds are located north about 3-6 miles at Tenmile and Eel lakes Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Scott, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7.5E, Sec 36 1989:  Just south of Mt. Washington Wilderness at end of a signed gravel side road leading north 3/4 mile from old McKenzie Pass Highway.  Several campground units at the lake, with toilets but no water.

lakes.name: Scott, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7.5E, Sec 36 1989:  Is actually part of Lower Scott Lake,  connected by a short, narrow waterway.  See "Lower Scott" description.

lakes.name: Scout Lake
lakes.description: This is a small lake managed by the city of Clatskanie. It is not scheduled for regular trout stocking but sometimes receives hatchery trout by special request. The last mile to this pond is via a forest road accessed by vehicle through a locked gate. Anglers can hike in from the gate or for vehicle access by checking out a gate key from the City of Clatskanie, located at 95 S. Nehalem. To get a key, it is best to call ahead at 503-728-2622. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Separation
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T17S, R6.5E, Sec 24 2006:  Hike-in lake, 7 miles into Three Sisters Wilderness Area of Willamette National Forest. Take Hwy 126 east from Eugene and turn onto Forest Rd. 2638 (Horse Creek Rd.) at the McKenzie Bridge. At Roney Creek, 8 miles up, take the trail to the lake. (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Serene
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Serene Lake Trail #512

lakes.name: Shadow
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T22S, R6E, Sec 5. 2012:  An 8-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 east to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake; continue on USFS Road 5897 past Betty Lake Trailhead to Shadow Bay Road (USFS Road 5896).  Shadow Lake is approximately 200 yards northeast of the Shadow Bay Road and before Shadow Bay Campground.  The Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590), which crosses USFS Road 5896, passes along the east side of Shadow Lake.

lakes.name: Sheep
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Red Lake Trail #719

lakes.name: Shellrock
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Shellrock Lake Trail #700

lakes.name: Sheridan Pond
lakes.description: Sheridan Pond is a 2 1/2-acre pond located on the edge of town. It provides excellent access for families and kids. Good parking. There is an outhouse provided.

lakes.name: Sherlock Gulch Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 10-acre reservoir located within the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php). The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually and have been caught up to 12”. The reservoir is muddy all year and anglers should target areas along the margin of the reservoir to catch fish. A fun side trip would be to stop by the public sunstone collection area or one of the private companies that specialize in hunting for sunstones, Oregon’s state gem. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Shevlin Pond
lakes.description: Shevlin Pond is a half-acre pond located among aspen trees in beautiful Shevlin Park (http://www.bendparksandrec.org/.docs/pg/10071) four miles west of Bend. It is open year-round but often freezes in the winter. There is a casting dock and good bank access around the pond making this is a great place to teach inexperienced anglers how to fish. Fishing here is restricted to juvenile anglers up to 17 years old. The daily limit bag is two trout with an 8-inch minimum length. The pond is stocked with rainbow trout from April to August, though fishing can be good through November. Facilities include restrooms and a wheelchair accessible bridge and dock. Shevlin Park also has hiking and mountain biking trails with scenic picnic areas throughout the park. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Shining
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. Shining Lake Trail #510

lakes.name: Shirley Lake
lakes.description: Shirley Lake is stocked with rainbow trout. It is a 4 acre lake, with a max depth of eleven feet.

lakes.name: Shorty's Pond
lakes.description: This is a 4-acre pond located within Ivor Davies Nature Park in the city of Molalla. It can be accessed by the fifth St. Trailhead across from Heckard Football Stadium.

lakes.name: Sid Luce Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 57-acre reservoir is located on Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php) approximately15 miles northwest of Plush, Oregon. The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout annually. It has a maximum depth of 33’ and remains muddy all year. It is accessible mid-May to October with a high clearance vehicle. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Siltcoos Lake
lakes.description: Siltcoos Lake, at 3,164 acres in size, is the largest lake on the central Oregon Coast. The lake is very popular for boating and fishing. The shoreline tends to be brushy and mostly in private ownership. For this reason, fishing from boats is prefered by most anglers.

lakes.name: Silver Creek Reservoir
lakes.description: A 65-acre reservoir on Silver Creek 2.5 miles south of Silverton on Hwy. 214.

lakes.name: Silver King
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Bull of the Woods Wilderness. Bagby Hot Springs Trail #544

lakes.name: Simtustus Lake
lakes.description: This is a deep reservoir formed by impoundment of the Deschutes River behind Pelton Dam about 6 miles west of Madras.Ore.. It is bordered by the Warm Springs Indian Reservation on the western shoreline. The dam backs up about 7 miles of the river, which is accessible almost exclusively by boat. The reservoir is open to fishing from late April through October. It is stocked with legal-sized rainbow trout before opening weekend and through the summer. A tribal fishing permit is required in addition to a state fishing license. There is a store, boat ramp and campground at Pelton Park. Boats are subject to a 10 m.p.h. speed limit on much of the reservoir, and boat landings on the west shore are prohibited, except at Indian Park. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Skookum
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Skookum Lake Trail #542

lakes.name: Skookum Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a 15-acre hike-in lake in the Umpqua River watershed. https://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/umpqua/recarea/?recid=63600 The lake is stocked annually with fingerling "Fishwich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Slide Lake
lakes.description: This is a small lake located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) between Summer Lake and Paisley. Anglers can access the trail from the Government Harvey Pass road coming from Summer Lake or via the High Line road coming from Paisley. Anglers follow a ½ mile trail to the lake, but can be rewarded with rainbow trout 16” or larger. The fish are stocked annually with fingerling rainbow trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Small Fry Lake
lakes.description: This is small pond next to Promontory Park and North Fork Reservoir near Estacada. Fishing restricted to youths 17 and under. Cleaning station, restroom nearby.

lakes.name: Smith
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 2 2012:  An 8-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  Travel east on Highway 58 to Westfir; proceed north on Aufderheider Scenic Byway (USFS Road 19) to USFS Road 1958 (Box Canyon Road); follow USFS Road 1958 to trailhead. Continue approximately 6 miles on Crossing Way Trail (USFS Trail 3307) to Smith Lake.

lakes.name: Smith Lake
lakes.description: This is a 35-acre lake located less than 2 miles north of Garibaldi, Ore., on Hwy. 101. Private land surrounds most of this lake. Bank access is limited from the highway side and requires a short walk over uneven terrain. Stocking is done at the dock located at Camp Magruder. It is possible to slide a small boat into the lake at a road crossing on the north end.

lakes.name: Smith Reservoir
lakes.description: Reservoir on the Willamette National Forest appr. 70 miles east of Springfield, Ore., This reservoir spans 170 acres and is part of the Carmen-Smith Hydroelectric Project, owned by the Eugene Water & Electric Board. Smith complex offers camping, picknicking and a boat launch maintained by the U.S. Forest Service. The Eugene Water & Electric Board is conducting a five-year construction project to retrofit, refurbish and upgrade capital equipment at its Carmen-Smith Hydroelectric Project. The work is being conducted in anticipation of a new federal operating license for the project. The capital construction projects planned for the 2017-2021 time frame will create significant public access constraints due primarily to safety concerns. In order to keep the public and construction personnel safe during the five-year project, EWEB and the Forest Service agreed to close access to Forest Road 730 at the Powerhouse. The closure will deny public access to Trail Bridge Campground, Smith Reservoir and Lake’s End Campground. The closure of the road to the public will begin in March 2017 and continue through 2021. More information (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/willamette/recreation/recarea/?recid=4400)

lakes.name: Snell Lake
lakes.description: Stocked with cutthroat and brook trout.  Maximum depth of 17 feet.

lakes.name: Snowshoe, Lower
lakes.description: Eighteen acrea lake stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Snowshoe, Upper
lakes.description: Thirty acre lake, stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: South Blue Lk
lakes.description: AccessDirections: Sky Lakes Wilderness Area hike in access only

lakes.name: South Lake
lakes.description: A 5-acre lake located within the Siuslaw National Forest. This lake is located on the ridgeline above Hebo Lake. Next to the lake is a U.S. Forest Service campground that is open year around. The last several miles of road to the lake are unimproved and rough. Use caution on this road. It is not advisable to take trailers to this lake. South Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!/?ss=110612&ttype=recarea&recid=42715&actid=79&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110400000000000&pnavid=110000000000000&cid=null&pname=Mt.+Hebo+Area+-+South+Lake+Campground)

lakes.name: South Yamhill River
lakes.description: The South Fork Yamhill from its confluence with the North Yamhill near McMinnville, upstream about 20 miles to Rock Creek near Grand Ronde is stocked with rainbow trout. Trout are released in multiple locations between Gold Creek Road Bridge and Willamina.Yamhill River Road runs parallel to much of this section and provides adequate turnouts and parking at several locations near the river. The remaining 15 miles of river open to trout fishing has some public access but also meanders across private lands. ODFW reminds anglers to be aware of and respectful toward private property rights along the river.

lakes.name: Sow Bug Lake
lakes.description: This small, three acre lake is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Spalding Pond
lakes.description: This is a 3-acre lake located on a historic mill site within the Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/rogue-siskiyou/). It has three ADA accessible fishing platforms and a surfaced accessible trail in a primitive setting appr. 21 miles southwest of Grants Pass, Ore. There is an accessible restroom and small campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/recarea/?recid=70678). There is a small pebble beach. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Sparks Lake
lakes.description: This is a 400-acre lake located about 25 miles west of Bend. It is a large, shallow, trout lake situated on the northwest edge of Mount Bachelor. It is the first of the high lakes visible from the Cascade Lakes Highway west of Bend and offers views of South Sister, Mount Bachelor, and Broken Top. Prior to 1997, Sparks Lake was a brook trout fishery and, although brookies are still available, the lake's featured species is now cutthroat trout. Restricted to fly angling only. Motorized and non-motorized boats are allowed on this lake. However, fishing from a motorized boat is prohibited. Due to the spectacular sceneray and proximity to Bend, Sparks Lake is very popular with canoeists and kayakers.. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Spaulding Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 20-acre reservoir located 30 miles southeast of Adel, Oregon within the Bureau of Land Management's Lakeview, Oregon District (http://www.blm.gov/or/districts/lakeview/index.php). A brown recreation sign is located on highway 140 at the turn-off for the reservoir. Another sign is located 11 miles down the dirt road. The reservoir is stocked with fingerling rainbow trout when water is present. Fish are currently about 10” and readily bite dark colored flies. Anglers should not attempt to drive to the reservoir if the road is muddy. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Spirit
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5E, Sec 24 2006:  About 5 miles west of Waldo Lake. Hike-in lake, one-quarter mile into Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge on Hwy 58, take Forest Rd. 24 (Salmon Creek Rd.) up to Forest Rd. 2421 (Black Creek Branch Rd.). After half a mile, turn north on Forest Rd. 2422 and go 7 miles to the trailhead. Trail 3584 leads the short distance to the lake.  (Oregon Fish Finder)

lakes.name: Spring
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T16S, R8E, Sec 8 1989:  In Three Sisters Wilderness, at northeast base of Sims Butte.  From Frog Camp trailhead on old McKenzie Highway Pass, take Trail 3528 for 0.7 mile, then take 0.5 mile long side trail to the right (west) which ends at Spring Lake.

lakes.name: Spring Lake
lakes.description: A 13-acre lake located on the east side of Hwy. 101 appr. 4 miles north of Garibaldi, Ore. Public access is limited to the highway shoulder. There is no public ramp. Fish stocking is done at the north end of the lake.

lakes.name: Spy
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 4 2012:  A 4-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Hwy (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake.  The Island Meadows Trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of Elk Lake. From Island Meadows Trailhead, continue west to the Pacific Crest Trail. Turn south on the Pacific Crest Trail and continue to USFS Trail 3517.  Continue on USFS Trail 3517 to McBee Trail (USFS Trail 3523); continue south on McBee Trail (USFS Trail 3523) for approximately 1.5 miles.  Spy Lake is visible (200 feet distant) from east side of trail.

lakes.name: Square
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T17S, R7.5E, Sec 15 2012:  A 4-acre lake in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene, take Highway 126 east past the community of McKenzie Bridge; turn southeast onto USFS Road 2643 (Foley Ridge Road). At the end of USFS Road 2643, take Substitute Point Trail (USFS Trail 3511); continue east on USFS Trail 3511 to junction of USFS Trail 3520; continue south on USFS Trail 3520 to Square Lake.  Square Lake is 200 yards north of Kidney Lake near Buck Meadows. An undeveloped trail continues southeast from springs at Buck Meadows Shelter into the timber and climbs moderately uphill to Square and Kidney Lakes. After ½ mile, the trail goes directly past the west shore of Square Lake.

lakes.name: Square Lake
lakes.description: Square Lake is 55 acre with a max depth of 43 feet, The lake is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Sru Lake
lakes.description: This is a small lake located appr. 21 miles southeast of Powers, Ore., on the Rogue-River-Siskiyou National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/main/rogue-siskiyou/home). Camping is available nearby at Sru Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/rogue-siskiyou/recreation/fishing/recarea/?recid=70098&actid=42). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: St. Louis Ponds
lakes.description: This is a 240-acre fishing complex of seven ponds owned and managed jointly by the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife and Marion County Parks Department. Site has a 2,300-foot paved ADA footpath with turnouts, fishing platforms, restrooms and picnic tables. More primitive trails are also available. A gate providing access to the last mile of dirt road to the complex is closed Oct. 1 - March 1, although anglers are still permitted to walk in to fish.

lakes.name: Stag Lake
lakes.description: This hike-in lake in the Diamond Peak Wilderness is 20 acres, with a maximum depth of 27 feet. Stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Strider Lake
lakes.description: This small, three acre lake has a maximum depth of 22 feet. Rainbow trout are stocked in this lake.

lakes.name: Sunnyside Pond
lakes.description: This is an 8-acre pond in Sunnyside County Park at Foster Reservoir about 7 miles east of Sweet Home, Ore.

lakes.name: Sunset Lake
lakes.description: A 107-acre lake located appr. 4 miles north of Gearhart, Ore., and west of Hwy. 101.Turn west off of the highway. Sunset Lake Park Next to this lake is a 3-acre park with 500 feet of frontage on the lake. Facilities include gravel boat ramp, gravel parking area, picnic tables, volleyball and basketball court, bank fishing area and fishing dock. (https://myodfw.com/recreation-report/fishing-report/northwest-zone)

lakes.name: Sunset No 1
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T18S, R7E, Sec 26 2012:  A 15-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Hwy (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake. The trailhead is across from the Ranger Station at the north end of Elk Lake. Go west to the Pacific Crest Trail; continue on the Pacific Crest Trail turn southwest; continue to USFS Trail 3515.  USFS Trail 3515 passes about 100 yards east of Sunset Lake (lake is visible if watching closely).

lakes.name: Sunset No 2
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S, R5.5E, Sec 29 1989:  On south border of Diamond Peak Wilderness, on old Summit Lake wagon road (USFS Road 380) immediately west of Sunrise Lake.  No trail or identifying sign and can't be seen from the road.

lakes.name: Surprise
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hook NF. Fish Lake Trail #717

lakes.name: Suttle Lake
lakes.description: This is a 240-acre lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) about 14 miles northwest of Sisters, Oregon, just off Hwy 20. In addition to fishing, this is a multi-use lake offers other recreational opportunities including, swimming, boating and water skiing. There are four boat ramps on the south shore of the lake, and U.S. Forest Service campgrounds on the northeast and southeast ends of the lake. The Lodge at Suttle Lake (http://thelodgeatsuttlelake.com/) offers lodging, cabin rentals, a restaurant and a marina with boat rentals and moorage. Suttle Lake supports populations of kokanee, brown and small numbers of rainbow trout . Kokanee is the most popular fishery and takes place from May through August. Trophy size brown trout are also available Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Sutton Lake
lakes.description: Sutton Lake is 107 acres in size and appears as two lakes connected by a short channel. Most of the shoreline is private ownership, except for the western shore, which is bounded by Hwy. 101 and a Forest Service day-use area with boat ramp. Sutton Campground (http://www.traveloregon.com/Explore-Oregon/Oregon-Coast/Outdoor-Recreation/Fishing/Fishing/Sutton-Campground.aspx)

lakes.name: Suzanne Lake
lakes.description: Ten acres, max depth 47 feet. Stocked brook trout.

lakes.name: Suzi
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Roaring River Wilderness. A bushwacking adventure from Shining Lake Trail #510.

lakes.name: Tahoe Lake
lakes.description: Tahoe Lake is accessible via forest roads in the Coast Range east of Tillamook. The lake offers public bank access around the entire lake on an unimproved trail. An unimproved boat launch is available for small boats. No ADA facilities or restrooms.

lakes.name: Taylor Lake
lakes.description: This is a 35-acre lake located adjacent to I-84 about a mile north of The Dalles, Oregon. This lake is open the entire year and stocked several times early in the year with legal- and trophy-sized rainbow trout. Most people fish from the bank along the north shore of the lake but float tubes and rubber rafts are also popular. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Teddy Bear
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S R04E Sec 06 Head of Coffee Pot Creek; Forest Service Rd #21 to 2118, to 479 to 2119 (lilly pads).

lakes.name: Teddy Lake
lakes.description: Thirty acre lake stocked with rainbow trout.

lakes.name: Tenas, Middle
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 2012:  A 1-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene take Highway 126, east (past the community of McKenzie Bridge) to McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242). Turn northeast Hwy 242; continue on to USFS Road 260 to Scott Lake; from Scott Lake Campground take USFS Trail 3502 past Benson Lake approximately one mile northwest to the Tenas Lakes.  Middle Tenas Lake is approximately 400 feet west of Lower Tenas Lake and 200 feet south of Upper Tenas Lake.

lakes.name: Tenas, Upper
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T15S, R7E, Sec 36 2012:  A 1-acre lake located in the Mt. Washington Wilderness in the Willamette National Forest.  From Eugene take Highway 126, east (past the community of McKenzie Bridge) to McKenzie Pass Highway (Hwy 242). Turn northeast on Hwy 242; continue on to USFS Road 260 to Scott Lake; from Scott Lake Campground take USFS Trail 3502 past Benson Lake approximately one mile northwest to the Tenas Lakes.  Upper Tenas Lake is approximately 150 yards west of the north end of Lower Tenas Lake and 200 feet north of Middle Tenas Lake.

lakes.name: Tenmile Lake
lakes.description: Two large lakes on the Oregon coast next to the town of Lakeside in Coos County. The lakes have 170 miles of shoreline, a surface area of 2,723 acres and an average depth of about 15 feet. They have numerous bays, arms and fingers that hold a variety of trout and warmwater fish species. These lakes are considered one of Oregon's premier warmwater fisheries. The lake is located just minutes from the Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area, (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72DTgBAjAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAObEnGA!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!/?navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&recid=42465&actid=&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&ss=110612&position=&ttype=recarea&pname=Siuslaw%20National%20Forest%20-%20Oregon%20Dunes%20National%20Recreation%20Area) which has numerous campgrounds, hiking trails, and other recreational amenities. Amenities: county park, two boat ramps, three marinas Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Thissel Pond
lakes.description: This is a 3-acre pond located just downstream from ODFW's Oregon Hatchery Research Center (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/fish/OHRC/) along Fall Creek. This pond dries up around mid-summer but supports a stocked rainbow trout fishery during the winter/spring season.

lakes.name: Thompson Valley Reservoir
lakes.description: Thisis a 2500-acre reservoir in a forested area of the Fremont-Winema NationalForest about 60 miles northeast of Klamath Falls, Oregon. This reservoir isopen year-around, and ice fishing is popular when snow allows access. Campingis available at two campgrounds (Thompson Reservoir and East Bay) and on thereservoir, and each has a boat ramp. The ramp at Thompson Valley Campground isgood for small boats, while the ramp at East Bay campground is better forlarger boats. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Three Creeks Lake
lakes.description: This is a 28-acre lake in the Deschutes National Forest. It is approximately 20 miles south of Sisters. Situated at an elevation of 6,500 feet in a beautiful cirque below Tam McCarthur Rim, this lake is often snowbound until May or June. Camping is available on the north shore of the lake at Driftwood Campground. Angling is excellent via trolling from a boat or canoe. Fly angling with float tubes is also popular. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Timber Linn Lake
lakes.description: This is an 11-acre pond in the 90-acre Timber Linn Memorial Park in Albany, Ore. This is a good lake for inexperience anglers because of easy access to the water.

lakes.name: Timothy Lake - Gone Creek Campground
lakes.description: Timothy Lake is one of the most popular family camping and fishing destinations in the Mt. Hood National Forest. The lake's south shore features four developed campgrounds and boat ramps. Three smaller, less developed campgrounds are found in the north. A trail system for hikers, mountain bikers and equestrians circles the lake. The Pacific Crest National Scenic Trail also traverses the area along the east side of the lake. Motorboats are allowed on Timothy Lake, although a 10 m.p.h. speed limit is in place. Lat./Long. Coordinates: 45.113633,-121.773577

lakes.name: Timothy Lake - Hoodview Campground
lakes.description: Timothy Lake is one of the most popular family camping and fishing destinations in the Mt. Hood National Forest. The lake's south shore features four developed campgrounds and boat ramps. Three smaller, less developed campgrounds are found in the north. A trail system for hikers, mountain bikers and equestrians circles the lake. The Pacific Crest National Scenic Trail also traverses the area along the east side of the lake. Motorboats are allowed on Timothy Lake, although a 10 m.p.h. speed limit is in place.

lakes.name: Timothy Lake - Pine Point Campground
lakes.description: Timothy Lake is one of the most popular family camping and fishing destinations in the Mt. Hood National Forest. The lake's south shore features four developed campgrounds and boat ramps. Three smaller, less developed campgrounds are found in the north. A trail system for hikers, mountain bikers and equestrians circles the lake. The Pacific Crest National Scenic Trail also traverses the area along the east side of the lake. Motorboats are allowed on Timothy Lake, although a 10 m.p.h. speed limit is in place.

lakes.name: Timpanogas
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5.5E, Sec 15 1989:  In Oregon Cascades Recreation Area, 3 miles south of Summit Lake at end of Road 399.  A several unit Forest Service campground (with water and toilets and bountiful mosquitoes) is at the lake.

lakes.name: Timpanogas, Lower
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T25S, R5.5, Sec 15 1989:  In Oregon Cascades Recreation Area, 100 yards southwest of Timpanogas Lake.  No identifying sign but can be seen from the road leading into the Timpanogas Lake Campground.

lakes.name: Torrey
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec. 33 2012:  A 67-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 east to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake; from North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue on the Taylor Burn Road for approximately 2.5 miles from North Waldo Campground, Take the Whig-Torrey Way Trail (USFS Trail 3581) for a mile west to Torrey Lake.  This large lake is easily visible along the north side of the trail.

lakes.name: Town Lake
lakes.description: This is a 9-acre impoundement located near the community of Woods, Ore. Most of the lake is surrounded by private land but public access is available along the west shore near the boat ramp, and includes the boat ramp itself. The bank to the lake is steep and brushy. An angling dock is located on the west side of the lake and is accessible by steep, unimproved trails that must be used with extreme caution.

lakes.name: Trail Bridge Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 120-acre reservoir about an hour east of Eugene, Oregon on Hwy. 126, within the Willamette National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/willamette). The reservoir has 46 walk-in/tent sites and 19 RV sites (without hookups), paved access roads, gravel boat launch, fishing platform, picnic tables, fire rings and one seasonal restroom. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Trillium Lake
lakes.description: Trillium is a 60-acre lake located appr. three miles east of Government Camp off of Hwy 26. This lake is popular for fishing, camping and photography, often clearly reflecting Mount Hood. Adjacent Trillium Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/mthood/recreation/camping-cabins/recarea/?recid=53634&actid=29) is administered by the Zigzag Ranger District of the Mount Hood National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/mthood/). The large campground features a seasonal boat ramp and wheelchair-accessible floating dock.

lakes.name: Trojan Pond
lakes.description: This is a 15-acre pond just east of Rainier on the north side of Hwy. 30 at the Trojan nuclear facility. The pond is located on the right side of the road as soon as you turn onto the Trojan Access Road.

lakes.name: Twin Lake - North
lakes.description: This is a 130-acre lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) about 45 miles southwest of Bend, Oregon. Open year round, it provides a fishery for 9 to 11-inch rainbow trout. It is a great family setting and provides good shelter from the wind. The lake offers good boat fishing, although use of motors is prohibited. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Twin Lake - South
lakes.description: This is a 99-acre natural lake located within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) about 60 miles southwest of Bend, Oregon off of the Cascade Lakes Highway. Consistent stocking of fingerlings and legal-size rainbow trout has resulted in an excellent fishery. Most anglers’ fish from boats and use bait. Depths of 15 to 30 feet are the best producers. Trolling slowly with a flasher and apiece of worm or egg or with a very small lure are productive methods throughout the season. Still fishing with eggs, worms, and a marshmallow or power bait far enough off the bottom to stay out of the weeds is also a popular method. South Twin Lake provides good shoreline access for both kids and adults. It provides a sheltered fishing venue when early season cold temperatures and wind become a problem on larger water bodies. Only non-motorized boats are allowed on this lake Camping is available at South Twin Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/recarea/?recid=39002). The South Twin Resort offers lodging, accommodations and supplies. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Twin Lakes - Umpqua
lakes.description: Two lakes located within the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua) in a secluded mountain setting. The larger of the two is a 12-acre lake, and the other is about half that size. Only the larger lake is stocked with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. There is a primitive campground in the area. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Twin, Lower
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: Not stocked Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Twin Lakes Trail #573

lakes.name: Twin, Upper
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: Not stocked Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Twin Lakes Trail #573

lakes.name: Veda
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Veda Lake Trail #673

lakes.name: Vee Lake
lakes.description: This 14-acre reservoir is located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) about 25 miles northeast of Lakeview. This reservoir can be accessed using NF Road #3615 then turning onto NF Road #3616 follow the signs to the Vee Lake trailhead. The reservoir is stocked with fingerling, catchable, and trophy rainbow trout annually and they have been caught to 14”. There are two campsites and a pit toilet and boat ramp for small boats or float tubes. Vegetation can be an issue for bank anglers during the summer. This is a great location to fish and view Hart Mountain National Antelope Refuge (http://www.fws.gov/sheldonhartmtn/Hart/index.html) or watch the stars during the summer. Access is limited to snowmobiles during the winter. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Vera
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T19S, R7E, Sec 10 2012:  A 1-acre lake located in the Three Sisters Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Bend, take the Cascade Lakes Hwy (Hwy 46) west to Elk Lake; from the south end of Elk Lake take the Doris Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3526); continue west on USFS Trail 3526 (USFS Trail 3526 will “zigzag” across the Pacific Crest Trail and continue on) to Vera Lake (south side of trail) approximately 200 feet from Questionmark Lake.

lakes.name: Vernonia Pond
lakes.description: Located at the north end of Vernonia, Ore., on Hwy 47, this waterbody was once the mill pond of the Oregon-American Lumber Company. Amenities: Primitive/walk-in camping area with fire pits, restrooms, drinking water, boat ramp (gas powered motors are not allowed), ADA wheelchair accessible fishing docks and restrooms, bank fishing access, and children’s playground area. There is an on-site bait shop during the spring and summer months. The Banks-Vernonia bike trail starts and ends at this site. Fishing is best in spring, as warmer temperatures promote growth of aquatic vegetation. The city of Vernonia owns this pond and charges $5 to park at the site.

lakes.name: Virginia
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T24S R04E Sec 30 Forest Service Road 21 to 2134, Spur 245 (1 mile northeast of Dome Rock).

lakes.name: Vivian
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R5.5E, Sec 17 2012:  A 12-acre lake located in the Diamond Peak Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 east to Salt Creek Falls; take Salt Creek Falls access road to trailhead (south along Fall Creek); continue south on USFS Trail 3662 past Fall Creek Falls; Vivian Lake is approximately 1 mile south of Fall Creek Falls.

lakes.name: Wahanna
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 32 2012:  A 51-acre lake in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake to North Waldo Campground; From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake; continue north on dirt road (Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514) to the Taylor Burn Campground; from Taylor Burn Campground, hike 1.5 miles south on the Wahanna Trail (USFS Trail 3583) to Wahanna Lake.

lakes.name: Waldo
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 20 (and other sections) Immediately east and south of Waldo Wilderness.  Follow the mosquitoes.

lakes.name: Wall
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Red Lake Trail #719

lakes.name: Walling Pond
lakes.description: This is an 8-acre privately owned pond that is open to the public for fishing. The pond, located in Salem, Oregon, is owned by the Walling family. The pond is located at the original site of their sand and gravel processing plant at the northeast corner of McGilchrist and 16th Streets, S.E.

lakes.name: Walter Wirth Lake
lakes.description: Walter Wirth Lake is a 20-acre pond located within the City of Salem's Cascades Gateway Park. The lake is named after Walter L. Wirth, a park superintendent for the City of Salem Parks Department from 1954-1958. Mill Stream, which runs through the park, has become a spawning area for fall Chinook salmon.

lakes.name: Walton Lake
lakes.description: This is a 19-acre lake located on within the Ochoco National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) approxiamtely 31 miles northeast of Prineville. This lake is open the entire year and is stocked prior to Memorial Day weekend and throughout the summer, with holdover trout reaching 16-18 inches. Snow limits access during the winter months.Boats are allowed but are restricted to electric motors. Camping is available at Walton Lake Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/recarea/centraloregon/recreation/recarea/?recid=38766), which has a boat ramp, picnic tables, vault toilets and drinking water. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Warm Springs Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 4,200-acre irrigation reservoir located about 49 miles east of Burns, Oregon via U.S.Hwy 20, then 14 miles south on a dirt road to the boat ramp. This reservoir is managed primarily for warmwater fish, but a few fingerling-sized rainbow trout are stocked in the spring. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Warner Lakes
lakes.description: This is a chain of lakes aligned from north to south throughout the Warner Basin of southeast Oregon. These lakes are located near the small towns of Adel, Oregon and Plush, Oregon. Crump and Hart Lakes usually have water, but many of the lakes to the north become dry for multiple years. These lakes have crappie and brown bullhead with the occasional redband trout. These lakes produce state record crappie when water persists for a few years. A campground with pit toilets is available at the ‘CCC camp’ on Hart Mountain National Antelope Refuge (http://www.fws.gov/sheldonhartmtn/Hart/index.html). Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Warner No 2
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T23S, R4E, Sec 30 1989:  In Middle Fork Willamette River drainage at north base of Warner Mountain, at end of Spur Road 442.  Quickest access is up Buck Creek Road, using Forest Service map showing road numbers, including Spur 442 which ends at the lake.  No identifying sign.

lakes.name: Waverly Lake
lakes.description: Waverly Lake is is located in an Albany City Park with restrooms and picnic facilities. In the summer months, paddle boats are available for rent.Other recreational activities are available nearby including paved bike trails, hiking and wildlife viewing. Waverly Lake offers excellent bank fishing opportunities and also has good access for disabled anglers, including a dock. There is a paved trail around the entire lake.

lakes.name: Webb
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R6E, Sec 9 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897), turning northeast to travel up the east side of Waldo Lake to North Waldo Campground; from the northeast end of Waldo Lake, Webb Lake is approximately 400 yards north; (Do not confuse with a bigger but shallower lake with a large island located 200 yards northwest of Webb Lake). A good map and compass are helpful even through lake is rather close to the Waldo Lake boat ramp.

lakes.name: Welcome
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Bull of the Woods Wilderness. Welcome Lakes Trail #554

lakes.name: Whig
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 33 2012:  A 14-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake; continue north on USFS Road 516 that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on USFS Road 514; The trailhead is located on Taylor Burn Road 100 yards west of the trail into Helen Lake.  Follow the Fields Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3579) south for approximately one mile to Fields Lake; from Fields Lake a well-defined but undeveloped trail drops downhill 300 yards southeast to Whig Lake.

lakes.name: Wickiup Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 11,200-acre reservoir within the Deschutes National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/centraloregon) created by the impoundment of the upper Deschutes River behind Wickiup Dam, approximately 40 miles southwest of Bend. This is one of Oregon's largest and most productive fisheries, offering self-sustaining populations of kokanee, brown trout, and rainbow trout. Both kokanee (up to 20 inches) and brown trout (up to 15 pounds) grow quite large here. This is primarily a boat fishery, although casting from shore is also popular. Depths range from 20 to 65 feet. Good boat ramps are available at Gull Point (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c5/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTSw8jAwgAykeaxcN4jhYG_h4eYX5hPgYwefy6w0H24dcPNgEHcDTQ9_PIz03VL8iNMMgycVQEAIzTHkw!/dl3/d3/L2dJQSEvUUt3QS9ZQnZ3LzZfME80MEkxVkFCOTBFMktTNUJIMjAwMDAwMDA!/?ss=110601&ttype=recarea&recid=38874&actid=29&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110130000000000&pnavid=110000000000000&cid=FSE_003705&pname=Central+Oregon+-+Gull+Point+Campground) and North Wickiup campgrounds at the mouth of the Deschutes arm, at West South Twin Campground (http://www.fs.usda.gov/wps/portal/fsinternet/!ut/p/c4/04_SB8K8xLLM9MSSzPy8xBz9CP0os3gDfxMDT8MwRydLA1cj72BTJw8jAwjQL8h2VAQAzHJMsQ!!?ss=110601&ttype=recarea&recid=39030&actid=31&navtype=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&position=BROWSEBYSUBJECT&navid=110000000000000&pnavid=&cid=null&pname=Central+Oregon+-+West+South+Twin+Campground) on the lower Deschutes Arm, Reservoir Campground on the southwest shore, and Wickiup Butte Campground on the southeast shore. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Williams
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T20S, R6E, Sec 20 2012:  A 3-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest. From Oakridge, follow Highway 58 to the Waldo Lake Road (USFS Road 5897); turn northeast and travel east side of Waldo Lake.  From North Waldo Campground on the northeast end of the lake, continue north on dirt road (USFS Road 516) that meets the Taylor Burn Road (USFS Road 514); continue west on USFS Road 514 to Taylor Burn Campground.  From the east side of Taylor Burn Campground, take the Williams Lake Trail (USFS Trail 3589) going north.

lakes.name: Williams
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF. Via Williams Lake Rd.

lakes.name: Willow Lake
lakes.description: Willow Lake is a popular southern Oregon reservoir which stores the waters of Willow Creek, a tributary of the South Fork of Big Butte Creek. The 52-foot high, earthfill dam was completed in 1952 by the Medford Water Commission to store water as part of their municipal water system. The lake is located at the base of Mt. McLoughlin on land owned by Jackson County. It is located approximately 7.5 miles east of Butte Falls and 43 miles northeast of Medfore, Ore. Jackson County has designated the surrounding 927 acres as a recreational area, which provides a number of amenities, including campsites, RV sites, cabins, picnic area, restrooms, and boat ramp. Amenities: Willow Lake County Park (http://www.co.jackson.or.us/Page.asp?NavID=3514) Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Wind
lakes.description: Stocking Frequency: 2 years Access Directions: Mt Hood NF

lakes.name: Windigo Lake, West
lakes.description: Windigo Lake is 5 acres with a maximum depth of 19 feet. The lake is stocked with brook trout.

lakes.name: Windy Lake, East
lakes.description: Fourteen acre lake stocked with brook trout. Max depth of 14 feet.

lakes.name: Windy Lake, North
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout. It is a 5 acre lake with a max depth of 27 feet

lakes.name: Windy Lake, South
lakes.description: Stocked with cutthroat. Eleven acre lake with a max depth of 28 feet.

lakes.name: Windy Lake, West
lakes.description: Stocked with brook trout. Sixteen acre lake with maximum depth of 18 feet.

lakes.name: Withers Lake
lakes.description: This is a 5-acre lake located on the Fremont-Winema National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/fremont-winema) between Summer Lake and Paisley. Anglers can access the trail from the Government Harvey Pass road coming from Summer Lake or via the High Line road coming from Paisley.  The lake is stocked with brown trout annually and has naturally produced brook trout.  Brown trout range from 8”-14” with a few larger fish.  This is a beautiful lake to float tube during the summer or fall.  There is a 100-yard walk from the parking area to the lake.  A pit toilet located at the parking area. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Woahink Lake
lakes.description: Woahink Lake is 820 acres in size and, at 72 feet deep, is the deepest lake on the central Oregon Coast. Much of the shoreline is in private residential ownership. About 15 percent lies within Honeyman State Park (http://www.oregonstateparks.org/park_134.php), which provides ample access, including a boat ramp, large day-use area and covered picnic shelters.

lakes.name: Wolf Lake - Umpqua
lakes.description: This is a small hike-in lake located in the Umpqua National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/umpqua/). It has horse corrals at the trailhead. The lake is stock annually with fingerling "FishWich" Rainbow Trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.eregulations.com/oregon/fishing/)

lakes.name: Yapoah Lake
lakes.description: High elevation lake stocked with brook trout. Approximately 10 acres with a max depth of 25 feet.

lakes.name: Yellowjacket Reservoir
lakes.description: This is a 35-acre impoundment located about 39 miles northwest of Burns, Oregon within the Malheur National Forest (http://www.fs.usda.gov/malheur). This impoundment is stocked in the spring with both legal-sized and fingerling-sized rainbow trout. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Yoran Lake
lakes.description: This hike-in lake is no longer stocked, but has naturally reproducing brook trout.  The lake spans 32 acres and has a maximum depth of 44 feet.

lakes.name: ZX Pond
lakes.description: This small pond is for kids under 14 and seniors over 65.  The pond is located on private property, but access is allowed for fishing.  The pond is stocked four times a year with rainbow trout.  Most of the fish are harvested within three weeks of stocking. Fishing Regulations (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/resources/fishing/reg_changes/index.asp)

lakes.name: Zircon
lakes.description: AccessDirections: T21S, R5.5E, Sec 13. 43.7457634N   122.0799995W                                                                 A 4-acre lake located in the Waldo Lake Wilderness in Willamette National Forest.  From Oakridge, take Highway 58 east to USFS Road 24 (Salmon Creek Road); continue on USFS Road 24 (where it becomes USFS Road 2421); turn left (north) on USFS Road 2422; follow USFS Road 2422 to end and to trailhead; follow USFS Trail 3576 east for approximately 1 mile to Waldo Lake; from Waldo Lake head north on the Jim Weaver Loop National Recreation Trail (USFS Trail 3590) for 2 miles to Elbow Lake.  Zircon Lake is ¾ mile southwest of Elbow Lake and 500 yards east of Sapphire Lake.  No trail or identifying sign (no simple access).  Suggest route is from Elbow Lake using a compass and good map.

Travel 3.0 miles to end of road at Deer Ck.  Trail head is across Deer Ck near railroad.  Old trail head appeared to have crossed no-longer-existing bridge across Deer Ck upstream of end of road.
